1069	    /// Whether the next button should be disabled (max constraint).
1070	    /// Checks against the **last** visible month instead of the first.
1071	    pub fn is_next_disabled(&self) -> bool {
1072	        if let Some(ref max) = self.ctx.max {
1073	            let last_offset = self.ctx.visible_months.saturating_sub(1);
1074	            let (month, year) = self.ctx.month_year_at_offset(last_offset);
1075	            let first_of_last = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year, NonZero::new(month).expect("month is 1-based"), nzu8(1));
1076	            let last_of_last = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(
1077	                year, NonZero::new(month).expect("month is 1-based"), NonZero::new(first_of_last.days_in_month()).expect("days_in_month is >= 1"),
1078	            );
1079	            last_of_last >= *max
1080	        } else {
1081	            false
1082	        }
1083	    }
1084	
1085	    /// The grid data: weeks and day labels.
1086	    pub fn weeks(&self) -> Vec<[CalendarDate; 7]> {
1087	        self.ctx.weeks()
1088	    }
1089	
1090	    /// Ordered day-of-week labels for the head row.
1091	    pub fn week_day_labels(&self) -> Vec<(Weekday, String)> {
1092	        self.ctx.week_day_labels()
1093	    }
1094	
1095	    /// The currently focused date.
1096	    pub fn focused_date(&self) -> &CalendarDate {
1097	        &self.ctx.focused_date
1098	    }
1099	
1100	    /// Whether the calendar grid has focus.
1101	    pub fn is_focused(&self) -> bool {
1102	        *self.state == State::Focused
1103	    }
1104	
1105	    fn state_name(&self) -> &'static str {
1106	        match self.state {
1107	            State::Idle    => "idle",
1108	            State::Focused => "focused",
1109	        }
1110	    }
1111	}
1112	
1113	impl ConnectApi for Api<'_> {
1114	    type Part = Part;
1115	
1116	    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap {
1117	        match &part {
1118	            Part::Root => self.root_attrs(),
1119	            Part::Header => self.header_attrs(),
1120	            Part::PrevTrigger => self.prev_trigger_attrs(),
1121	            Part::NextTrigger => self.next_trigger_attrs(),
1122	            Part::Heading => self.heading_text_attrs(),
1123	            Part::Grid => self.grid_attrs(),
1124	            Part::GridGroup => self.grid_group_attrs(),
1125	            Part::HeadRow => self.head_row_attrs(),
1126	            Part::HeadCell { day } => self.head_cell_attrs(day),
1127	            Part::Row { week_index } => self.row_attrs(*week_index),
1128	            Part::Cell { date } => self.cell_attrs(date),
1129	            Part::CellTrigger { date } => self.cell_trigger_attrs(date),
1130	        }
1131	    }
1132	}
1133	```
1134	
1135	## 2. Anatomy
1136	
1137	```text
1138	Calendar (en-US, January 2024)
1139	└── Root                                  data-ars-scope="calendar"
1140	    ├── Header                            data-ars-part="header"
1141	    │   ├── PrevTrigger                   aria-label="Previous month"
1142	    │   ├── HeadingText                   "January 2024"
1143	    │   └── NextTrigger                   aria-label="Next month"
1144	    └── Grid                              role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading"
1145	        ├── HeadRow
1146	        │   ├── HeadCell "Su"             scope="col"  abbr="Sunday"
1147	        │   ├── HeadCell "Mo"             scope="col"  abbr="Monday"
1148	        │   ├── … (7 cells total)
1149	        │   └── HeadCell "Sa"             scope="col"  abbr="Saturday"
1150	        ├── Row (week 1)
1151	        │   ├── Cell > CellTrigger "31"   data-ars-outside-month  aria-disabled="true"
1152	        │   ├── Cell > CellTrigger "1"    tabindex="0"  aria-selected="false"
1153	        │   ├── …
1154	        │   └── Cell > CellTrigger "6"
1155	        ├── Row (week 2) … Row (week 5)
1156	        └── Row (week 6)
1157	            └── Cell > CellTrigger "3"    data-ars-outside-month
1158	```
1159	
1160	| Part          | HTML Element | Key Attributes                                                               |
1161	| ------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1162	| `Root`        | `<div>`      | `data-ars-scope="calendar"`, `data-ars-part="root"`, `data-ars-state`        |
1163	| `Header`      | `<div>`      | `data-ars-part="header"`                                                     |
1164	| `PrevTrigger` | `<button>`   | `aria-label`, `tabindex="-1"`                                                |
1165	| `NextTrigger` | `<button>`   | `aria-label`, `tabindex="-1"`                                                |
1166	| `Heading`     | `<span>`     | `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`                                   |
1167	| `GridGroup`   | `<div>`      | `role="group"`, `aria-label` (only when `visible_months > 1`)                |
1168	| `Grid`        | `<table>`    | `role="grid"`, `aria-labelledby`                                             |
1169	| `HeadRow`     | `<tr>`       | —                                                                            |
1170	| `HeadCell`    | `<th>`       | `scope="col"`, `abbr`                                                        |
1171	| `Row`         | `<tr>`       | —                                                                            |
1172	| `Cell`        | `<td>`       | `role="gridcell"`, `aria-selected`                                           |
1173	| `CellTrigger` | `<button>`   | `tabindex`, `aria-label`, `aria-selected`, `aria-disabled`, `data-ars-today` |
1174	
1175	**Multi-month anatomy** (`visible_months=2`):
1176	
1177	```text
1178	Calendar (en-US, January–February 2024, visible_months=2)
1179	└── Root                                  data-ars-scope="calendar"
1180	    ├── Header                            data-ars-part="header"
1181	    │   ├── PrevTrigger                   aria-label="Previous 2 months"
1182	    │   ├── Heading                       "January – February 2024"
1183	    │   └── NextTrigger                   aria-label="Next 2 months"
1184	    └── GridGroup                         role="group"  aria-label="January – February 2024"
1185	        ├── Grid[0]                       role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading-0"
1186	        │   ├── Heading[0]               "January 2024"  (visually hidden)
1187	        │   ├── HeadRow … HeadCell×7
1188	        │   └── Row×6 > Cell > CellTrigger
1189	        └── Grid[1]                       role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading-1"
1190	            ├── Heading[1]               "February 2024"  (visually hidden)
1191	            ├── HeadRow … HeadCell×7
1192	            └── Row×6 > Cell > CellTrigger
1193	```
1194	
1195	When `visible_months == 1`, anatomy is identical to the single-month tree above (no `GridGroup` wrapper).
1196	
1197	## 3. Accessibility
1198	
1199	- Each selected cell: `aria-selected="true"`.
1200	- Screen readers announce selection state changes via `aria-live` region.
1201	- In Multiple mode: `aria-multiselectable="true"` on the grid.
1202	
1203	### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties
1204	
1205	| Element                | Role/Attribute          | Details                                                                                    |
1206	| ---------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
1207	| `Grid`                 | `role="grid"`           | `aria-labelledby` points to `HeadingText`                                                  |
1208	| `HeadCell`             | `<th scope="col">`      | `abbr` attribute holds full weekday name                                                   |
1209	| `Cell`                 | `role="gridcell"`       | `aria-selected` on selected date(s)                                                        |
1210	| `CellTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label` = full date string; `aria-disabled` on disabled/unavailable                   |
1211	| `HeadingText`          | —                       | `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic="true"` for month changes                              |
1212	| `PrevTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Previous month"` (or `"Previous N months"` when step > 1)                     |
1213	| `NextTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Next month"` (or `"Next N months"` when step > 1)                             |
1214	| `GridGroup`            | `role="group"`          | Wraps multiple grids; `aria-label` = month range. Not rendered when `visible_months == 1`. |
1215	| `Grid[n]` (per-grid)   | `role="grid"`           | `aria-labelledby` → per-grid heading `{id}-heading-{n}`                                    |
1216	| Per-grid `HeadingText` | `id="{id}-heading-{n}"` | Visually hidden per-grid heading. No `aria-live` (only the main heading has it).           |
1217	
1218	### 3.2 Keyboard Interaction
1219	
1220	| Key               | Action                                       |
1221	| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
1222	| `ArrowLeft`       | Move focus to previous day (next day in RTL) |
1223	| `ArrowRight`      | Move focus to next day (previous day in RTL) |
1224	| `ArrowUp`         | Move focus to same day in previous week      |
1225	| `ArrowDown`       | Move focus to same day in next week          |
1226	| `Home`            | Move focus to start of current week          |
1227	| `End`             | Move focus to end of current week            |
1228	| `PageUp`          | Move focus to same day in previous month     |
1229	| `PageDown`        | Move focus to same day in next month         |
1230	| `Shift+PageUp`    | Move focus to same day in previous year      |
1231	| `Shift+PageDown`  | Move focus to same day in next year          |
1232	| `Enter` / `Space` | Select the focused date                      |
1233	
1234	### 3.3 Focus Management
1235	
1236	- Only one cell in the grid is in the tab order at a time (`tabindex="0"`); all others have `tabindex="-1"`.
1237	- Arrow keys move the focused cell roving within the grid.
1238	- When focus moves to a date outside the visible month, the visible month auto-scrolls.
1239	- The PrevTrigger and NextTrigger buttons have `tabindex="-1"` and are activated by click only; they do not participate in the roving tab order of the grid.
1240	- When `visible_months > 1` and arrow-key navigation crosses a month boundary that remains within the visible range, the view does **not** scroll. Only when focus leaves the entire visible range does the calendar paginate.
1241	
1242	### 3.4 Screen Reader Announcements
1243	
1244	- Month navigation: HeadingText has `aria-live="polite"` so screen readers announce "February 2024" when the month changes.
1245	- When `visible_months > 1`, month navigation announces the full range (e.g., "January – February 2024"). Per-grid headings do NOT have `aria-live` to avoid duplicate announcements.
1246	- Date selection: Cell's `aria-selected` change is announced.
1247	- Each CellTrigger has an `aria-label` like "January 15, 2024, Monday" for unambiguous date reading.
1248	
1249	## 4. Internationalization
1250	
1251	### 4.1 Messages
1252	
1253	```rust
1254	// ars-i18n/src/calendar/i18n.rs
1255	
1256	/// Locale-specific labels for the Calendar component.
1257	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
1258	pub struct Messages {
1259	    /// Accessible label for the previous month navigation button.
1260	    pub prev_month_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1261	    /// Accessible label for the next month navigation button.
1262	    pub next_month_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1263	    /// Label for prev button when navigating multiple months.
1264	    /// The `usize` parameter is the step count.
1265	    pub prev_page_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1266	    /// Label for next button when navigating multiple months.
1267	    /// The `usize` parameter is the step count.
1268	    pub next_page_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1269	    /// Separator between month names in multi-month range heading (e.g., " – ").
1270	    pub month_range_separator: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1271	}
1272	// Month names, weekday names, and abbreviations are resolved from the IntlBackend
1273	// (via `month_long_name()`, `wd.short_label()`, etc.) — not stored in Messages.
1274	
1275	impl Default for Messages {
1276	    fn default() -> Self {
1277	        Self {
1278	            prev_month_label: MessageFn::static_str("Previous month"),
1279	            next_month_label: MessageFn::static_str("Next month"),
1280	            prev_page_label: MessageFn::new(|count, _locale| format!("Previous {} months", count)),
1281	            next_page_label: MessageFn::new(|count, _locale| format!("Next {} months", count)),
1282	            month_range_separator: MessageFn::static_str(" \u{2013} "),
1283	        }
1284	    }
1285	}
1286	
1287	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
1288	```
1289	
1290	**Locale-aware week start:**
1291	
1292	| Locale                                               | First Day of Week |
1293	| ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
1294	| `en-US`, `en-CA`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-TW`          | Sunday            |
1295	| `ar-SA`, `he-IL`                                     | Sunday            |
1296	| `en-GB`, `de-DE`, `fr-FR`, `es-ES`, `ru-RU`, `it-IT` | Monday            |
1297	| `fa-IR`                                              | Saturday          |
1298	
1299	The `first_day_of_week` prop overrides the locale default when provided.
1300	
1301	### 4.2 First Day of Week
1302	
1303	1. Default derived from locale via ICU4X `WeekCalculator` (US: Sunday, most of Europe: Monday, parts of Middle East: Saturday).
1304	2. Overridable via `first_day_of_week: Option<Weekday>` prop.
1305	3. In RTL locales, the calendar grid is mirrored horizontally (week starts from the right).
1306	4. DateField weekday segment typeahead matches locale-appropriate day names.
1307	
1308	### 4.3 Week Numbers
1309	
1310	1. Optional `show_week_numbers: bool` prop (default `false`).
1311	2. Week numbering follows locale convention: ISO 8601 (most of world) vs US week numbering via ICU4X `WeekCalculator`.
1312	3. Week number column header has `aria-label="Week number"` (localized).
1313	4. Each week number cell has `aria-label="Week {n}"` (localized).
1314	5. Week numbers are not focusable or selectable by default.
1315	
1316	## 5. Variant: Multiple Selection
1317	
1318	Calendar supports multiple non-contiguous date selection in addition to the default single-date mode.
1319	
1320	### 5.1 Additional Props
1321	
1322	```rust,no_check
1323	/// Added to Props.
1324	pub selection_mode: CalendarSelectionMode,
1325	/// Maximum number of dates that can be selected.
1326	pub max_selected: Option<usize>,
1327	
1328	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default)]
1329	pub enum CalendarSelectionMode {
1330	    /// Select a single date (default behavior).
1331	    #[default]
1332	    Single,
1333	    /// Select multiple non-contiguous dates.
1334	    Multiple,
1335	}
1336	```
1337	
1338	### 5.2 Additional Context
1339	
1340	```rust,no_check
1341	/// Extended CalendarContext for multi-selection.
1342	/// When selection_mode is Multiple:
1343	pub selected_dates: BTreeSet<CalendarDate>,
1344	```
1345	
1346	### 5.3 Additional Events
1347	
1348	```rust,no_check
1349	/// Added to Calendar Event enum.
1350	/// Toggle a date in/out of the selection set.
1351	ToggleDate(CalendarDate),
1352	```
1353	
1354	### 5.4 Behavior
1355	
1356	| Action   | Click                 | Shift+Click                                 |
1357	| -------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
1358	| Multiple | Toggle date selection | Select range from last-clicked to this date |
1359	
1360	When `max_selected` is set, the `ToggleDate` transition guards against `selected_dates.len() >= max_selected`. Excess selections are silently prevented.
1361	
1362	### 5.5 Keyboard
1363	
1364	| Key           | Behavior                                  |
1365	| ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
1366	| Space / Enter | Toggle focused date in/out of selection   |
1367	| Shift+Space   | Select range from anchor to focused       |
1368	| Ctrl+A        | Select all visible dates in current month |
1369	
1370	## 6. Library Parity
1371	
1372	> Compared against: React Aria (`Calendar`).
1373	
1374	### 6.1 Props
1375	
1376	| Feature                    | ars-ui                                | React Aria                    | Notes                                                             |
1377	| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
1378	| Controlled value           | `value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>` | `value: DateValue`            | Equivalent                                                        |
1379	| Default value              | `default_value`                       | `defaultValue`                | Equivalent                                                        |
1380	| Focused value (controlled) | --                                    | `focusedValue`                | ars-ui has `focused_date` in Context but not as a controlled prop |
1381	| Default focused value      | --                                    | `defaultFocusedValue`         | ars-ui derives from value/today                                   |
1382	| Min/max                    | `min`, `max`                          | `minValue`, `maxValue`        | Equivalent                                                        |
1383	| Unavailable predicate      | `is_date_unavailable`                 | `isDateUnavailable`           | Equivalent                                                        |
1384	| Disabled                   | `disabled`                            | `isDisabled`                  | Equivalent                                                        |
1385	| Read-only                  | `readonly`                            | `isReadOnly`                  | Equivalent                                                        |
1386	| Invalid                    | --                                    | `isInvalid`                   | Calendar has no `invalid` prop                                    |
1387	| First day of week          | `first_day_of_week`                   | `firstDayOfWeek`              | Equivalent                                                        |
1388	| Visible months             | `visible_months`                      | `visibleDuration: { months }` | Equivalent concept, different shape                               |
1389	| Page behavior              | `page_behavior`                       | `pageBehavior`                | Equivalent                                                        |
1390	| Auto-focus                 | --                                    | `autoFocus`                   | Not present in ars-ui                                             |
1391	| Create calendar            | --                                    | `createCalendar`              | ars-ui uses `CalendarSystem` enum instead                         |
1392	
1393	**Gaps:** None worth adopting. `autoFocus` is an adapter concern (the adapter can set focus on mount). `focusedValue`/`defaultFocusedValue` as controlled props add complexity without clear benefit since focus is a transient UI state. `isInvalid` on Calendar is not needed since Calendar does not participate in form validation directly.
1394	
1395	### 6.2 Anatomy
1396	
1397	| Part         | ars-ui        | React Aria                    | Notes                                                    |
1398	| ------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
1399	| Root         | `Root`        | `Calendar`                    | Equivalent                                               |
1400	| Header       | `Header`      | --                            | ars-ui groups nav controls; React Aria uses flat buttons |
1401	| Prev trigger | `PrevTrigger` | `Button` (prev)               | Equivalent                                               |
1402	| Next trigger | `NextTrigger` | `Button` (next)               | Equivalent                                               |
1403	| Heading      | `Heading`     | `Heading`                     | Equivalent                                               |
1404	| Grid         | `Grid`        | `CalendarGrid`                | Equivalent                                               |
1405	| Grid group   | `GridGroup`   | --                            | ars-ui wraps multi-month grids                           |
1406	| Head row     | `HeadRow`     | `CalendarGridHeader`          | Equivalent                                               |
1407	| Head cell    | `HeadCell`    | `CalendarHeaderCell`          | Equivalent                                               |
1408	| Body         | --            | `CalendarGridBody`            | ars-ui has no explicit body part                         |
1409	| Row          | `Row`         | --                            | Implicit in React Aria                                   |
1410	| Cell         | `Cell`        | `CalendarCell` (outer)        | Equivalent                                               |
1411	| Cell trigger | `CellTrigger` | `CalendarCell` (inner button) | ars-ui splits cell and trigger                           |
1412	
1413	**Gaps:** None. The anatomy is structurally equivalent with minor naming differences.
1414	
1415	### 6.3 Events
1416	
1417	| Callback     | ars-ui             | React Aria      | Notes      |
1418	| ------------ | ------------------ | --------------- | ---------- |
1419	| Value change | `SelectDate` event | `onChange`      | Equivalent |
1420	| Focus change | `FocusDate` event  | `onFocusChange` | Equivalent |
1421	
1422	**Gaps:** None.
1423	
1424	### 6.4 Features
1425	
1426	| Feature                 | ars-ui                    | React Aria           |
1427	| ----------------------- | ------------------------- | -------------------- |
1428	| Single date selection   | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1429	| Multiple date selection | Yes (variant)             | No                   |
1430	| Min/max constraints     | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1431	| Unavailable dates       | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1432	| Multi-month display     | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1433	| Page behavior           | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1434	| Locale-aware week start | Yes                       | Yes                  |
1435	| Week numbers            | Yes                       | No                   |
1436	| RTL support             | Yes                       | Yes (via locale)     |
1437	| Keyboard navigation     | Yes (WAI-ARIA Grid)       | Yes (WAI-ARIA Grid)  |
1438	| Calendar system support | Yes (CalendarSystem enum) | Yes (createCalendar) |
1439	
1440	**Gaps:** None. ars-ui is a superset (week numbers, multiple selection variant).
1441	
1442	### 6.5 Summary
1443	
1444	- **Overall:** Full parity.
1445	- **Divergences:** ars-ui supports week numbers and multi-date selection which React Aria does not. React Aria uses `createCalendar` factory while ars-ui uses a `CalendarSystem` enum.
1446	- **Recommended additions:** None.

    /// Whether the next button should be disabled (max constraint).
    /// Checks against the **last** visible month instead of the first.
    pub fn is_next_disabled(&self) -> bool {
        if let Some(ref max) = self.ctx.max {
            let last_offset = self.ctx.visible_months.saturating_sub(1);
            let (month, year) = self.ctx.month_year_at_offset(last_offset);
            let first_of_last = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year, NonZero::new(month).expect("month is 1-based"), nzu8(1));
            let last_of_last = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(
                year, NonZero::new(month).expect("month is 1-based"), NonZero::new(first_of_last.days_in_month()).expect("days_in_month is >= 1"),
            );
            last_of_last >= *max
        } else {
            false
        }
    }

    /// The grid data: weeks and day labels.
    pub fn weeks(&self) -> Vec<[CalendarDate; 7]> {
        self.ctx.weeks()
    }

    /// Ordered day-of-week labels for the head row.
    pub fn week_day_labels(&self) -> Vec<(Weekday, String)> {
        self.ctx.week_day_labels()
    }

    /// The currently focused date.
    pub fn focused_date(&self) -> &CalendarDate {
        &self.ctx.focused_date
    }

    /// Whether the calendar grid has focus.
    pub fn is_focused(&self) -> bool {
        *self.state == State::Focused
    }

    fn state_name(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self.state {
            State::Idle    => "idle",
            State::Focused => "focused",
        }
    }
}

impl ConnectApi for Api<'_> {
    type Part = Part;

    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap {
        match &part {
            Part::Root => self.root_attrs(),
            Part::Header => self.header_attrs(),
            Part::PrevTrigger => self.prev_trigger_attrs(),
            Part::NextTrigger => self.next_trigger_attrs(),
            Part::Heading => self.heading_text_attrs(),
            Part::Grid => self.grid_attrs(),
            Part::GridGroup => self.grid_group_attrs(),
            Part::HeadRow => self.head_row_attrs(),
            Part::HeadCell { day } => self.head_cell_attrs(day),
            Part::Row { week_index } => self.row_attrs(*week_index),
            Part::Cell { date } => self.cell_attrs(date),
            Part::CellTrigger { date } => self.cell_trigger_attrs(date),
        }
    }
}

## 2. Anatomy

```text
Calendar (en-US, January 2024)
└── Root                                  data-ars-scope="calendar"
    ├── Header                            data-ars-part="header"
    │   ├── PrevTrigger                   aria-label="Previous month"
    │   ├── HeadingText                   "January 2024"
    │   └── NextTrigger                   aria-label="Next month"
    └── Grid                              role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading"
        ├── HeadRow
        │   ├── HeadCell "Su"             scope="col"  abbr="Sunday"
        │   ├── HeadCell "Mo"             scope="col"  abbr="Monday"
        │   ├── … (7 cells total)
        │   └── HeadCell "Sa"             scope="col"  abbr="Saturday"
        ├── Row (week 1)
        │   ├── Cell > CellTrigger "31"   data-ars-outside-month  aria-disabled="true"
        │   ├── Cell > CellTrigger "1"    tabindex="0"  aria-selected="false"
        │   ├── …
        │   └── Cell > CellTrigger "6"
        ├── Row (week 2) … Row (week 5)
        └── Row (week 6)
            └── Cell > CellTrigger "3"    data-ars-outside-month
```

| Part          | HTML Element | Key Attributes                                                               |
| ------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Root`        | `<div>`      | `data-ars-scope="calendar"`, `data-ars-part="root"`, `data-ars-state`        |
| `Header`      | `<div>`      | `data-ars-part="header"`                                                     |
| `PrevTrigger` | `<button>`   | `aria-label`, `tabindex="-1"`                                                |
| `NextTrigger` | `<button>`   | `aria-label`, `tabindex="-1"`                                                |
| `Heading`     | `<span>`     | `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`                                   |
| `GridGroup`   | `<div>`      | `role="group"`, `aria-label` (only when `visible_months > 1`)                |
| `Grid`        | `<table>`    | `role="grid"`, `aria-labelledby`                                             |
| `HeadRow`     | `<tr>`       | —                                                                            |
| `HeadCell`    | `<th>`       | `scope="col"`, `abbr`                                                        |
| `Row`         | `<tr>`       | —                                                                            |
| `Cell`        | `<td>`       | `role="gridcell"`, `aria-selected`                                           |
| `CellTrigger` | `<button>`   | `tabindex`, `aria-label`, `aria-selected`, `aria-disabled`, `data-ars-today` |

**Multi-month anatomy** (`visible_months=2`):

```text
Calendar (en-US, January–February 2024, visible_months=2)
└── Root                                  data-ars-scope="calendar"
    ├── Header                            data-ars-part="header"
    │   ├── PrevTrigger                   aria-label="Previous 2 months"
    │   ├── Heading                       "January – February 2024"
    │   └── NextTrigger                   aria-label="Next 2 months"
    └── GridGroup                         role="group"  aria-label="January – February 2024"
        ├── Grid[0]                       role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading-0"
        │   ├── Heading[0]               "January 2024"  (visually hidden)
        │   ├── HeadRow … HeadCell×7
        │   └── Row×6 > Cell > CellTrigger
        └── Grid[1]                       role="grid"  aria-labelledby="{id}-heading-1"
            ├── Heading[1]               "February 2024"  (visually hidden)
            ├── HeadRow … HeadCell×7
            └── Row×6 > Cell > CellTrigger
```

When `visible_months == 1`, anatomy is identical to the single-month tree above (no `GridGroup` wrapper).

## 3. Accessibility

- Each selected cell: `aria-selected="true"`.
- Screen readers announce selection state changes via `aria-live` region.
- In Multiple mode: `aria-multiselectable="true"` on the grid.

### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties

| Element                | Role/Attribute          | Details                                                                                    |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Grid`                 | `role="grid"`           | `aria-labelledby` points to `HeadingText`                                                  |
| `HeadCell`             | `<th scope="col">`      | `abbr` attribute holds full weekday name                                                   |
| `Cell`                 | `role="gridcell"`       | `aria-selected` on selected date(s)                                                        |
| `CellTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label` = full date string; `aria-disabled` on disabled/unavailable                   |
| `HeadingText`          | —                       | `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic="true"` for month changes                              |
| `PrevTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Previous month"` (or `"Previous N months"` when step > 1)                     |
| `NextTrigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Next month"` (or `"Next N months"` when step > 1)                             |
| `GridGroup`            | `role="group"`          | Wraps multiple grids; `aria-label` = month range. Not rendered when `visible_months == 1`. |
| `Grid[n]` (per-grid)   | `role="grid"`           | `aria-labelledby` → per-grid heading `{id}-heading-{n}`                                    |
| Per-grid `HeadingText` | `id="{id}-heading-{n}"` | Visually hidden per-grid heading. No `aria-live` (only the main heading has it).           |

### 3.2 Keyboard Interaction

| Key               | Action                                       |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ArrowLeft`       | Move focus to previous day (next day in RTL) |
| `ArrowRight`      | Move focus to next day (previous day in RTL) |
| `ArrowUp`         | Move focus to same day in previous week      |
| `ArrowDown`       | Move focus to same day in next week          |
| `Home`            | Move focus to start of current week          |
| `End`             | Move focus to end of current week            |
| `PageUp`          | Move focus to same day in previous month     |
| `PageDown`        | Move focus to same day in next month         |
| `Shift+PageUp`    | Move focus to same day in previous year      |
| `Shift+PageDown`  | Move focus to same day in next year          |
| `Enter` / `Space` | Select the focused date                      |

### 3.3 Focus Management

- Only one cell in the grid is in the tab order at a time (`tabindex="0"`); all others have `tabindex="-1"`.
- Arrow keys move the focused cell roving within the grid.
- When focus moves to a date outside the visible month, the visible month auto-scrolls.
- The PrevTrigger and NextTrigger buttons have `tabindex="-1"` and are activated by click only; they do not participate in the roving tab order of the grid.
- When `visible_months > 1` and arrow-key navigation crosses a month boundary that remains within the visible range, the view does **not** scroll. Only when focus leaves the entire visible range does the calendar paginate.

### 3.4 Screen Reader Announcements

- Month navigation: HeadingText has `aria-live="polite"` so screen readers announce "February 2024" when the month changes.
- When `visible_months > 1`, month navigation announces the full range (e.g., "January – February 2024"). Per-grid headings do NOT have `aria-live` to avoid duplicate announcements.
- Date selection: Cell's `aria-selected` change is announced.
- Each CellTrigger has an `aria-label` like "January 15, 2024, Monday" for unambiguous date reading.

## 4. Internationalization

### 4.1 Messages

```rust
// ars-i18n/src/calendar/i18n.rs

/// Locale-specific labels for the Calendar component.
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct Messages {
    /// Accessible label for the previous month navigation button.
    pub prev_month_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
    /// Accessible label for the next month navigation button.
    pub next_month_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
    /// Label for prev button when navigating multiple months.
    /// The `usize` parameter is the step count.
    pub prev_page_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
    /// Label for next button when navigating multiple months.
    /// The `usize` parameter is the step count.
    pub next_page_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
    /// Separator between month names in multi-month range heading (e.g., " – ").
    pub month_range_separator: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
}
// Month names, weekday names, and abbreviations are resolved from the IntlBackend
// (via `month_long_name()`, `wd.short_label()`, etc.) — not stored in Messages.

impl Default for Messages {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            prev_month_label: MessageFn::static_str("Previous month"),
            next_month_label: MessageFn::static_str("Next month"),
            prev_page_label: MessageFn::new(|count, _locale| format!("Previous {} months", count)),
            next_page_label: MessageFn::new(|count, _locale| format!("Next {} months", count)),
            month_range_separator: MessageFn::static_str(" \u{2013} "),
        }
    }
}

impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
```

**Locale-aware week start:**

| Locale                                               | First Day of Week |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `en-US`, `en-CA`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-TW`          | Sunday            |
| `ar-SA`, `he-IL`                                     | Sunday            |
| `en-GB`, `de-DE`, `fr-FR`, `es-ES`, `ru-RU`, `it-IT` | Monday            |
| `fa-IR`                                              | Saturday          |

The `first_day_of_week` prop overrides the locale default when provided.

### 4.2 First Day of Week

1. Default derived from locale via ICU4X `WeekCalculator` (US: Sunday, most of Europe: Monday, parts of Middle East: Saturday).
2. Overridable via `first_day_of_week: Option<Weekday>` prop.
3. In RTL locales, the calendar grid is mirrored horizontally (week starts from the right).
4. DateField weekday segment typeahead matches locale-appropriate day names.

### 4.3 Week Numbers

1. Optional `show_week_numbers: bool` prop (default `false`).
2. Week numbering follows locale convention: ISO 8601 (most of world) vs US week numbering via ICU4X `WeekCalculator`.
3. Week number column header has `aria-label="Week number"` (localized).
4. Each week number cell has `aria-label="Week {n}"` (localized).
5. Week numbers are not focusable or selectable by default.

## 5. Variant: Multiple Selection

Calendar supports multiple non-contiguous date selection in addition to the default single-date mode.

### 5.1 Additional Props

```rust,no_check
/// Added to Props.
pub selection_mode: CalendarSelectionMode,
/// Maximum number of dates that can be selected.
pub max_selected: Option<usize>,

#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default)]
pub enum CalendarSelectionMode {
    /// Select a single date (default behavior).
    #[default]
    Single,
    /// Select multiple non-contiguous dates.
    Multiple,
}
```

### 5.2 Additional Context

```rust,no_check
/// Extended CalendarContext for multi-selection.
/// When selection_mode is Multiple:
pub selected_dates: BTreeSet<CalendarDate>,
```

### 5.3 Additional Events

```rust,no_check
/// Added to Calendar Event enum.
/// Toggle a date in/out of the selection set.
ToggleDate(CalendarDate),
```

### 5.4 Behavior

| Action   | Click                 | Shift+Click                                 |
| -------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Multiple | Toggle date selection | Select range from last-clicked to this date |

When `max_selected` is set, the `ToggleDate` transition guards against `selected_dates.len() >= max_selected`. Excess selections are silently prevented.

### 5.5 Keyboard

| Key           | Behavior                                  |
| ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| Space / Enter | Toggle focused date in/out of selection   |
| Shift+Space   | Select range from anchor to focused       |
| Ctrl+A        | Select all visible dates in current month |

## 6. Library Parity

> Compared against: React Aria (`Calendar`).

### 6.1 Props

| Feature                    | ars-ui                                | React Aria                    | Notes                                                             |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Controlled value           | `value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>` | `value: DateValue`            | Equivalent                                                        |
| Default value              | `default_value`                       | `defaultValue`                | Equivalent                                                        |
| Focused value (controlled) | --                                    | `focusedValue`                | ars-ui has `focused_date` in Context but not as a controlled prop |
| Default focused value      | --                                    | `defaultFocusedValue`         | ars-ui derives from value/today                                   |
| Min/max                    | `min`, `max`                          | `minValue`, `maxValue`        | Equivalent                                                        |
| Unavailable predicate      | `is_date_unavailable`                 | `isDateUnavailable`           | Equivalent                                                        |
| Disabled                   | `disabled`                            | `isDisabled`                  | Equivalent                                                        |
| Read-only                  | `readonly`                            | `isReadOnly`                  | Equivalent                                                        |
| Invalid                    | --                                    | `isInvalid`                   | Calendar has no `invalid` prop                                    |
| First day of week          | `first_day_of_week`                   | `firstDayOfWeek`              | Equivalent                                                        |
| Visible months             | `visible_months`                      | `visibleDuration: { months }` | Equivalent concept, different shape                               |
| Page behavior              | `page_behavior`                       | `pageBehavior`                | Equivalent                                                        |
| Auto-focus                 | --                                    | `autoFocus`                   | Not present in ars-ui                                             |
| Create calendar            | --                                    | `createCalendar`              | ars-ui uses `CalendarSystem` enum instead                         |

**Gaps:** None worth adopting. `autoFocus` is an adapter concern (the adapter can set focus on mount). `focusedValue`/`defaultFocusedValue` as controlled props add complexity without clear benefit since focus is a transient UI state. `isInvalid` on Calendar is not needed since Calendar does not participate in form validation directly.

### 6.2 Anatomy

| Part         | ars-ui        | React Aria                    | Notes                                                    |
| ------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Root         | `Root`        | `Calendar`                    | Equivalent                                               |
| Header       | `Header`      | --                            | ars-ui groups nav controls; React Aria uses flat buttons |
| Prev trigger | `PrevTrigger` | `Button` (prev)               | Equivalent                                               |
| Next trigger | `NextTrigger` | `Button` (next)               | Equivalent                                               |
| Heading      | `Heading`     | `Heading`                     | Equivalent                                               |
| Grid         | `Grid`        | `CalendarGrid`                | Equivalent                                               |
| Grid group   | `GridGroup`   | --                            | ars-ui wraps multi-month grids                           |
| Head row     | `HeadRow`     | `CalendarGridHeader`          | Equivalent                                               |
| Head cell    | `HeadCell`    | `CalendarHeaderCell`          | Equivalent                                               |
| Body         | --            | `CalendarGridBody`            | ars-ui has no explicit body part                         |
| Row          | `Row`         | --                            | Implicit in React Aria                                   |
| Cell         | `Cell`        | `CalendarCell` (outer)        | Equivalent                                               |
| Cell trigger | `CellTrigger` | `CalendarCell` (inner button) | ars-ui splits cell and trigger                           |

**Gaps:** None. The anatomy is structurally equivalent with minor naming differences.

### 6.3 Events

| Callback     | ars-ui             | React Aria      | Notes      |
| ------------ | ------------------ | --------------- | ---------- |
| Value change | `SelectDate` event | `onChange`      | Equivalent |
| Focus change | `FocusDate` event  | `onFocusChange` | Equivalent |

**Gaps:** None.

### 6.4 Features

| Feature                 | ars-ui                    | React Aria           |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------- | -------------------- |
| Single date selection   | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Multiple date selection | Yes (variant)             | No                   |
| Min/max constraints     | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Unavailable dates       | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Multi-month display     | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Page behavior           | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Locale-aware week start | Yes                       | Yes                  |
| Week numbers            | Yes                       | No                   |
| RTL support             | Yes                       | Yes (via locale)     |
| Keyboard navigation     | Yes (WAI-ARIA Grid)       | Yes (WAI-ARIA Grid)  |
| Calendar system support | Yes (CalendarSystem enum) | Yes (createCalendar) |

**Gaps:** None. ars-ui is a superset (week numbers, multiple selection variant).

### 6.5 Summary

- **Overall:** Full parity.
- **Divergences:** ars-ui supports week numbers and multi-date selection which React Aria does not. React Aria uses `createCalendar` factory while ars-ui uses a `CalendarSystem` enum.
- **Recommended additions:** None.

## Appendix: Testing

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use ars_core::service::Service;

    fn make_service() -> Service<Machine> {
        Service::new(Props {
            id: "test-cal".into(),
            value: None,
            default_value: None,
            min: None,
            max: None,
            disabled: false,
            readonly: false,
            is_date_unavailable: None,
            first_day_of_week: None,
            is_range: false,
            is_rtl: false,
            visible_months: 1,
            page_behavior: PageBehavior::Visible,
            today: CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(15)),
        }, Env::default(), Default::default())
    }

    #[test]
    fn initial_state_is_idle() {
        let svc = make_service();
        assert_eq!(*svc.state(), State::Idle);
    }

    #[test]
    fn focus_in_transitions_to_focused() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        svc.send(Event::FocusIn);
        assert_eq!(*svc.state(), State::Focused);
    }

    #[test]
    fn arrow_right_advances_focused_date() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        svc.send(Event::FocusIn);
        let before = svc.context().focused_date.clone();
        svc.send(Event::KeyDown { key: KeyboardKey::ArrowRight });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().focused_date, before.add_days(1));
    }

    #[test]
    fn arrow_up_moves_back_one_week() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        svc.send(Event::FocusIn);
        let before = svc.context().focused_date.clone();
        svc.send(Event::KeyDown { key: KeyboardKey::ArrowUp });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().focused_date, before.add_days(-7));
    }

    #[test]
    fn select_date_updates_value() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        let date = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(20));
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: date.clone() });
        assert_eq!(*svc.context().value.get(), Some(date));
    }

    #[test]
    fn next_month_advances_visible_month() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1);
        svc.send(Event::NextMonth);
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn prev_month_goes_back() {
        let mut svc = make_service();
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1);
        svc.send(Event::PrevMonth);
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 12);
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_year, 2023);
    }

    #[test]
    fn disabled_calendar_ignores_events() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            disabled: true,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate {
            date: CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(20)),
        });
        assert_eq!(*svc.context().value.get(), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn range_mode_first_click_sets_start() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            is_range: true,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        let date = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(10));
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: date.clone() });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_start, Some(date));
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_end, None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn range_mode_second_click_completes_range() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            is_range: true,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        let start = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(10));
        let end = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(20));
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: start.clone() });
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: end.clone() });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_start, Some(start));
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_end, Some(end));
    }

    #[test]
    fn range_mode_swaps_if_end_before_start() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            is_range: true,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        let later = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(20));
        let earlier = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(5));
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: later.clone() });
        svc.send(Event::SelectDate { date: earlier.clone() });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_start, Some(earlier));
        assert_eq!(svc.context().range_end, Some(later));
    }

    #[test]
    fn min_max_clamps_focused_date() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            min: Some(CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(10))),
            max: Some(CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(25))),
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        svc.send(Event::FocusDate {
            date: CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(1)),
        });
        assert_eq!(
            svc.context().focused_date,
            CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(10)),
        );
    }

    #[test]
    fn rtl_arrow_keys_reversed() {
        let mut svc = Service::new(Props {
            is_rtl: true,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        svc.send(Event::FocusIn);
        let before = svc.context().focused_date.clone();
        // In RTL, ArrowLeft should advance (next day).
        svc.send(Event::KeyDown { key: KeyboardKey::ArrowLeft });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().focused_date, before.add_days(1));
    }

    // ── Multi-month tests ───────────────────────────────────────────

    fn make_multi_month_service(months: usize, behavior: PageBehavior) -> Service<Machine> {
        Service::new(Props {
            visible_months: months,
            page_behavior: behavior,
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default())
    }

    #[test]
    fn multi_month_weeks_for_returns_correct_months() {
        let svc = make_multi_month_service(2, PageBehavior::Visible);
        let weeks0 = svc.context().weeks_for(0);
        let weeks1 = svc.context().weeks_for(1);
        // First grid should contain January dates, second should contain February.
        // Check a mid-month date in each grid.
        assert_eq!(weeks0[2][3].month.get(), 1); // mid-January
        assert_eq!(weeks1[2][3].month.get(), 2); // mid-February
    }

    #[test]
    fn next_month_advances_by_visible_months_in_visible_mode() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(3, PageBehavior::Visible);
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1);
        svc.send(Event::NextMonth);
        // Should jump +3: January → April.
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 4);
    }

    #[test]
    fn next_month_advances_by_1_in_single_mode() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(3, PageBehavior::Single);
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1);
        svc.send(Event::NextMonth);
        // Should jump +1: January → February.
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn prev_month_steps_back_by_visible_months() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(2, PageBehavior::Visible);
        // Advance to March first.
        svc.send(Event::NextMonth); // Jan → Mar (step 2)
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 3);
        svc.send(Event::PrevMonth); // Mar → Jan (step -2)
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1);
    }

    #[test]
    fn sync_visible_does_not_scroll_when_focus_in_range() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(2, PageBehavior::Visible);
        // visible_month = 1 (Jan), visible_months = 2, so Jan+Feb visible.
        // Focus on a Feb date — should NOT scroll.
        let feb_date = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(2), nzu8(10));
        svc.send(Event::FocusDate { date: feb_date });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 1); // Still January
    }

    #[test]
    fn sync_visible_scrolls_when_focus_leaves_range() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(2, PageBehavior::Visible);
        // visible range is Jan+Feb. Focus on March — should scroll.
        let mar_date = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(3), nzu8(5));
        svc.send(Event::FocusDate { date: mar_date });
        assert_eq!(svc.context().visible_month, 3); // Scrolled to March
    }

    #[test]
    fn is_in_visible_range_checks_all_months() {
        let svc = make_multi_month_service(3, PageBehavior::Visible);
        let jan = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(1), nzu8(15));
        let feb = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(2), nzu8(15));
        let mar = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(3), nzu8(15));
        let apr = CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(4), nzu8(1));
        assert!(svc.context().is_in_visible_range(&jan));
        assert!(svc.context().is_in_visible_range(&feb));
        assert!(svc.context().is_in_visible_range(&mar));
        assert!(!svc.context().is_in_visible_range(&apr));
    }

    #[test]
    fn month_year_at_offset_wraps_year() {
        let mut svc = make_multi_month_service(3, PageBehavior::Visible);
        // Set visible to November 2024.
        svc.send(Event::SetMonth { month: 11 });
        let (m, y) = svc.context().month_year_at_offset(2);
        assert_eq!(m, 1);    // January
        assert_eq!(y, 2025); // Next year
    }

    #[test]
    fn is_next_disabled_checks_last_visible_month() {
        let svc = Service::new(Props {
            visible_months: 2,
            page_behavior: PageBehavior::Visible,
            max: Some(CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2024, nzu8(2), nzu8(28))),
            ..make_service().props().clone()
        }, Env::default(), Default::default());
        let api = Machine::connect(
            svc.state(), svc.context(), svc.props(), &|_| {},
        );
        // visible_month=1 (Jan), visible_months=2 → last visible is Feb.
        // max = Feb 28. Last day of Feb = Feb 29 (2024 is a leap year) ≥ Feb 28.
        // But Feb 28 is the max and the last of the last visible month
        // includes Feb 29 which is ≥ max, so next should be disabled.
        assert!(api.is_next_disabled());
    }
}
```

300	    /// Adds a date duration to this value.
301	    ///
302	    /// # Errors
303	    ///
304	    /// Returns an error if the arithmetic result cannot be represented in this

306	    pub fn add(&self, duration: DateDuration) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
307	        let current = temporal_date_for(self)?;
308	
309	        let next = current
310	            .add(&duration.to_temporal()?, Some(Overflow::Constrain))
311	            .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
312	
313	        build_from_iso_parts(
314	            self.calendar,
315	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).year(),
316	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).month(),
317	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).day(),
318	        )
319	        .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))
320	    }

329	        self.add(DateDuration {
330	            years: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.years, "year")?,
331	            months: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.months, "month")?,
332	            weeks: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.weeks, "week")?,
333	            days: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.days, "day")?,
334	        })

342	    /// in this calendar.
343	    pub fn set(&self, fields: &CalendarDateFields) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
344	        let current = temporal_date_for(self)?;
345	
346	        let replacement = temporal_calendar_fields(fields)?;
347	
348	        let next = current
349	            .with(replacement, Some(Overflow::Reject))
350	            .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
351	
352	        build_from_iso_parts(
353	            self.calendar,
354	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).year(),
355	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).month(),
356	            next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).day(),
357	        )
358	        .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))
359	    }

361	    /// Cycles a date field by the requested amount.
362	    ///
363	    /// # Errors
364	    ///
365	    /// Returns an error if applying the requested cycle would produce an
366	    /// unrepresentable date.
367	    pub fn cycle(
368	        &self,
369	        field: DateField,
370	        amount: i32,
371	        options: CycleOptions,
372	    ) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
373	        if amount == 0 {
374	            return Ok(self.clone());
375	        }
376	
377	        match field {
378	            DateField::Year => self.add(DateDuration {
379	                years: amount,
380	                ..DateDuration::default()
381	            }),
382	
383	            DateField::Month => {
384	                if options.wrap {
385	                    let minimum_month = i32::from(self.minimum_month_in_year());
386	                    let maximum_month = i32::from(self.calendar.months_in_year(self));
387	
388	                    let months_in_year = maximum_month - minimum_month + 1;
389	
390	                    let next_month = (i32::from(self.month) - minimum_month + amount)
391	                        .rem_euclid(months_in_year)
392	                        + minimum_month;
393	
394	                    let current = temporal_date_for(self)?;
395	
396	                    let next = current
397	                        .with(
398	                            CalendarFields::new()
399	                                .with_optional_month(Some(u8::try_from(next_month).map_err(
400	                                    |_| {
401	                                        CalendarError::Arithmetic(String::from(
402	                                            "month cycle overflow",
403	                                        ))
404	                                    },
405	                                )?))
406	                                .with_optional_day(Some(self.day)),
407	                            Some(Overflow::Reject),
408	                        )
409	                        .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
410	
411	                    build_from_iso_parts(
412	                        self.calendar,
413	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).year(),
414	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).month(),
415	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).day(),
416	                    )
417	                    .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))
418	                } else {
419	                    self.add(DateDuration {
420	                        months: amount,
421	                        ..DateDuration::default()
422	                    })
423	                }
424	            }
425	
426	            DateField::Day => {
427	                if options.wrap {
428	                    let minimum_day = i32::from(self.minimum_day_in_month());
429	                    let maximum_day = i32::from(self.calendar.days_in_month(self));
430	
431	                    let days_in_month = maximum_day - minimum_day + 1;
432	
433	                    let next_day = (i32::from(self.day) - minimum_day + amount)
434	                        .rem_euclid(days_in_month)
435	                        + minimum_day;
436	
437	                    let current = temporal_date_for(self)?;
438	
439	                    let next = current
440	                        .with(
441	                            CalendarFields::new().with_optional_day(Some(
442	                                u8::try_from(next_day).map_err(|_| {
443	                                    CalendarError::Arithmetic(String::from("day cycle overflow"))
444	                                })?,
445	                            )),
446	                            Some(Overflow::Reject),
447	                        )
448	                        .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
449	
450	                    build_from_iso_parts(
451	                        self.calendar,
452	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).year(),
453	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).month(),
454	                        next.with_calendar(Calendar::ISO).day(),
455	                    )
456	                    .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))
457	                } else {
458	                    self.add(DateDuration {
459	                        days: amount,
460	                        ..DateDuration::default()
461	                    })
462	                }
463	            }
464	        }
465	    }
466	
467	    /// Converts this date into a different calendar system.
468	    ///
469	    /// # Errors
470	    ///
471	    /// Returns an error if the target calendar is unsupported or the
472	    /// conversion fails in the underlying calendar engine.
473	    pub fn to_calendar(&self, target: CalendarSystem) -> Result<Self, CalendarConversionError> {
474	        build_from_iso_parts(target, self.iso_year, self.iso_month, self.iso_day)
475	    }
476	
477	    /// Compares two dates chronologically using their canonical ISO slots.
478	    #[must_use]
479	    pub fn compare(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {
480	        (self.iso_year, self.iso_month, self.iso_day).cmp(&(
481	            other.iso_year,
482	            other.iso_month,
483	            other.iso_day,
484	        ))
485	    }
486	
487	    /// Returns the canonical ISO 8601 representation of this date.
488	    #[must_use]
489	    pub fn to_iso8601(&self) -> String {
490	        format!(
491	            "{}-{:02}-{:02}",
492	            format_iso_year(self.iso_year),
493	            self.iso_month,
494	            self.iso_day
495	        )
496	    }
497	
498	    /// Returns the ISO weekday for this date.
499	    #[must_use]
500	    pub fn weekday(&self) -> Weekday {
501	        match temporal_iso_date(self.iso_year, self.iso_month, self.iso_day)
502	            .expect("stored ISO date is always valid")
503	            .day_of_week()
504	        {
505	            1 => Weekday::Monday,
506	            2 => Weekday::Tuesday,
507	            3 => Weekday::Wednesday,
508	            4 => Weekday::Thursday,
509	            5 => Weekday::Friday,
510	            6 => Weekday::Saturday,
511	            _ => Weekday::Sunday,
512	        }
513	    }
514	
515	    /// Adds whole ISO days.
516	    ///
517	    /// # Errors
518	    ///
519	    /// Returns an error if the resulting date cannot be represented.
520	    pub fn add_days(&self, days: i32) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
521	        self.add(DateDuration {
522	            days,
523	            ..DateDuration::default()
524	        })
525	    }
526	
527	    /// Returns the day distance to another date.
528	    ///
529	    /// # Errors
530	    ///
531	    /// Returns an error if either date cannot be converted into the internal
532	    /// calendar engine.
533	    pub fn days_until(&self, other: &Self) -> Result<i32, CalendarError> {
534	        let start = temporal_iso_date(self.iso_year, self.iso_month, self.iso_day)
535	            .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
536	
537	        let end = temporal_iso_date(other.iso_year, other.iso_month, other.iso_day)
538	            .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
539

600	    fn scope() -> &'static str;
601	
602	    /// Returns the string name of this part (e.g. `"root"`, `"trigger"`).
603	    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
604	
605	    /// Returns all parts defined for this component.
606	    fn all() -> Vec<Self>;
607	
608	    /// Returns the canonical `data-ars-scope` and `data-ars-part` attrs for this part.
609	    fn data_attrs(&self) -> [(HtmlAttr, &'static str); 2] {
610	        [
611	            (HtmlAttr::Data("ars-scope"), Self::scope()),
612	            (HtmlAttr::Data("ars-part"), self.name()),
613	        ]
614	    }
615	}
616	
617	/// Produces HTML attributes for each component part based on current machine state.
618	///
619	/// Implementors bridge the machine's state, context, and props into concrete
620	/// [`AttrMap`] values that framework adapters spread onto DOM elements.
621	pub trait ConnectApi {
622	    /// The component part enum this API produces attributes for.
623	    type Part: ComponentPart;
624	
625	    /// Returns the attribute map for the given part.
626	    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Self::Part) -> AttrMap;
627	}
628	
629	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
630	// Env — adapter-resolved environment context
631	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
632	
633	/// Runtime render mode resolved by the adapter before initializing a machine.
634	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
635	pub enum RenderMode {
636	    /// The component is rendering in a normal client runtime.
637	    #[default]
638	    Client,
639	
640	    /// The component is rendering HTML on the server.
641	    Server,
642	
643	    /// The component is hydrating server-rendered HTML on the client.
644	    Hydrating,
645	}
646	
647	impl RenderMode {
648	    /// Returns whether this mode is server-side rendering.
649	    #[must_use]
650	    pub const fn is_server(self) -> bool {
651	        matches!(self, Self::Server)
652	    }
653	
654	    /// Returns whether this mode is client hydration.
655	    #[must_use]
656	    pub const fn is_hydrating(self) -> bool {
657	        matches!(self, Self::Hydrating)
658	    }
659	}
660	
661	/// Adapter-resolved environment context passed to [`Machine::init`].
662	///
663	/// The adapter reads these values from `ArsProvider` / `ArsContext` and passes
664	/// them to framework-agnostic core code. Core component code **never** calls
665	/// framework hooks (`use_locale()`, `use_intl_backend()`, `use_context()`) —
666	/// all environment values arrive through this struct.
667	///
668	/// Non-date-time components ignore `intl_backend` (it defaults to [`StubIntlBackend`]).
669	pub struct Env {
670	    /// The resolved locale from `ArsProvider`.
671	    pub locale: Locale,
672	
673	    /// Calendar/locale data provider for date-time formatting.
674	    /// Defaults to [`StubIntlBackend`] (English-only, zero dependencies).
675	    pub intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>,
676	
677	    /// Runtime render mode for server, hydration, or normal client rendering.
678	    pub render_mode: RenderMode,
679	}
680	
681	impl Env {
682	    /// Creates an environment with the supplied locale and internationalization
683	    /// backend in normal client render mode.
684	    #[must_use]
685	    pub fn new(locale: Locale, intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>) -> Self {
686	        Self {
687	            locale,
688	            intl_backend,
689	            render_mode: RenderMode::Client,
690	        }
691	    }
692	
693	    /// Returns this environment with a different runtime render mode.
694	    #[must_use]
695	    pub const fn with_render_mode(mut self, render_mode: RenderMode) -> Self {
696	        self.render_mode = render_mode;
697	        self
698	    }
699	}
700	
701	impl Debug for Env {
702	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
703	        f.debug_struct("Env")
704	            .field("locale", &self.locale)
705	            .field("intl_backend", &"Arc(..)")
706	            .field("render_mode", &self.render_mode)
707	            .finish()
708	    }
709	}
710	
711	impl Default for Env {
712	    fn default() -> Self {
713	        Self {
714	            locale: ars_i18n::locales::en_us(),
715	            intl_backend: Arc::new(StubIntlBackend),
716	            render_mode: RenderMode::Client,
717	        }
718	    }
719	}
720	
721	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
722	// Machine trait
723	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
724	
725	/// Defines a component as a finite state machine.
726	///
727	/// A `Machine` declares the component's state type, event type, internal context,
728	/// props, and connect API. It provides pure functions for initialization, transition
729	/// logic, and DOM attribute generation — with no framework dependency.
730	pub trait Machine: Sized + 'static {
731	    /// The state type representing the machine's current configuration.
732	    type State: Clone + Debug + PartialEq;
733	
734	    /// The event type that triggers state transitions.
735	    type Event: Clone + Debug;
736	
737	    /// Internal context accumulated across transitions (e.g. focused index, scroll offset).
738	    type Context: Clone + Debug;
739	
740	    /// External configuration passed in by the parent component.
741	    type Props: Clone + PartialEq + HasId;
742	
743	    /// Per-component i18n messages type.
744	    type Messages: ComponentMessages + Clone + Default + 'static;
745	
746	    /// Typed identifier for the named effect intents this machine emits.
747	    ///
748	    /// Each component declares its own enum (e.g. `popover::Effect`,
749	    /// `dialog::Effect`) — every variant is a stable identifier the
750	    /// compiler treats as the single source of truth. Adapters that
751	    /// route on names use exhaustive `match effect.name { … }` so a new
752	    /// variant added in the component forces every adapter to update.
753	    ///
754	    /// Machines that never emit named effects use [`NoEffect`]; the
755	    /// uninhabited type makes `PendingEffect::<_>::named(...)`
756	    /// uncallable for that machine.
757	    ///
758	    /// Prototypes and internal test fixtures can use
759	    /// `type Effect = &'static str` — `&'static str` already implements
760	    /// [`EffectName`] — but production machines should prefer a
761	    /// bespoke enum so authoring code, adapter dispatch, and test
762	    /// fixtures share one typed identifier. See [`EffectName`] for the
763	    /// full bound rationale.
764	    type Effect: EffectName;
765	
766	    /// The connect API type that produces attributes from current state.
767	    type Api<'a>: ConnectApi
768	    where
769	        Self: 'a;
770	
771	    /// Initialize the machine from props and adapter-resolved environment values,
772	    /// returning initial state and context.
773	    ///
774	    /// The adapter resolves `env` (locale, ICU provider) and `messages` from
775	    /// `ArsProvider` context before calling this method. Core code never calls
776	    /// framework hooks — all environment values arrive as parameters.
777	    fn init(
778	        props: &Self::Props,
779	        env: &Env,
780	        messages: &Self::Messages,
781	    ) -> (Self::State, Self::Context);
782	
783	    /// Evaluates an event against the current state, context, and props.
784	    ///
785	    /// Returns `Some(plan)` to apply a transition or `None` to ignore the event.
786	    fn transition(
787	        state: &Self::State,
788	        event: &Self::Event,
789	        context: &Self::Context,
790	        props: &Self::Props,
791	    ) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Self>>;
792	
793	    /// Creates the connect API for producing DOM attributes from the current state.
794	    fn connect<'a>(
795	        state: &'a Self::State,
796	        context: &'a Self::Context,
797	        props: &'a Self::Props,
798	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Self::Event),
799	    ) -> Self::Api<'a>;
