35	### 1.2 Events
36	
37	```rust
38	/// Events for the Calendar component.
39	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
40	pub enum Event {
41	    /// Move keyboard focus to a specific date.
42	    FocusDate {
43	        /// The date to focus on.
44	        date: CalendarDate,
45	    },
46	    /// User selected a date (click or Enter/Space on focused cell).
47	    SelectDate {
48	        /// The date to select.
49	        date: CalendarDate,
50	    },
51	    /// Navigate to the next month.
52	    NextMonth,
53	    /// Navigate to the previous month.
54	    PrevMonth,
55	    /// Navigate to the next year.
56	    NextYear,
57	    /// Navigate to the previous year.
58	    PrevYear,
59	    /// Jump to a specific month (1-based).
60	    SetMonth {
61	        /// The month to jump to.
62	        month: u8,
63	    },
64	    /// Jump to a specific year.
65	    SetYear {
66	        /// The year to jump to.
67	        year: i32,
68	    },
69	    /// Grid received focus.
70	    FocusIn,
71	    /// Focus left the grid entirely.
72	    FocusOut,
73	    /// Keyboard event on the grid.
74	    KeyDown {
75	        /// The key that was pressed.
76	        key: KeyboardKey,
77	    },
78	}
79	```
80	
81	### 1.3 Context
82	
83	```rust
84	/// Context for the Calendar component.
85	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
86	pub struct Context {
87	    /// Selected date (single-select mode).
88	    pub value: Bindable<Option<CalendarDate>>,
89	    /// The date that currently holds keyboard focus within the grid.
90	    pub focused_date: CalendarDate,
91	    /// The month currently displayed (1-based).
92	    pub visible_month: u8,
93	    /// The year currently displayed.
94	    pub visible_year: i32,
95	    /// Minimum selectable date.
96	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
97	    /// Maximum selectable date.
98	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
99	    /// Locale for week start, month/day names.
100	    pub locale: Locale,
101	    /// Resolved translatable messages.
102	    pub messages: Messages,
103	    /// Intl backend for locale-dependent formatting (month/weekday names, etc.).
104	    pub intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>,
105	    /// Override for first day of week (falls back to locale default).
106	    pub first_day_of_week: Weekday,
107	    /// Right-to-left layout.
108	    pub is_rtl: bool,
109	    /// Disabled state — ignores all interactive events.
110	    pub disabled: bool,
111	    /// Read-only state — can focus/navigate but not select.
112	    pub readonly: bool,
113	    /// Static list of unavailable dates (pre-computed from Props.is_date_unavailable
114	    /// for the currently visible month range). Refreshed on month navigation.
115	    pub unavailable_dates: Vec<CalendarDate>,
116	    /// Reference to the user-provided predicate from Props.is_date_unavailable.
117	    /// Used by `is_date_unavailable()` for dynamic evaluation beyond the static list.
118	    pub is_date_unavailable_fn: Option<fn(&CalendarDate) -> bool>,
119	    /// Component IDs.
120	    pub ids: ComponentIds,
121	    /// Number of months displayed side-by-side.
122	    pub visible_months: usize,
123	    /// Navigation step size.
124	    pub page_behavior: PageBehavior,
125	    /// Whether to display ISO week numbers.
126	    pub show_week_numbers: bool,
127	}
128	
129	impl Context {
130	    /// Whether the given date is the currently selected value.
131	    pub fn is_selected(&self, date: &CalendarDate) -> bool {
132	        self.value.get().as_ref() == Some(date)
133	    }
134	
135	    /// Whether a date is disabled (outside min/max or globally disabled).
136	    pub fn is_date_disabled(&self, date: &CalendarDate) -> bool {
137	        if self.disabled { return true; }
138	        if let Some(ref min) = self.min {
139	            if date < min { return true; }
140	        }
141	        if let Some(ref max) = self.max {
142	            if date > max { return true; }
143	        }
144	        false
145	    }
146	
147	    /// Whether a date is marked unavailable by the user-provided predicate.
148	    /// Checks both the static `unavailable_dates` list and the dynamic
149	    /// `is_date_unavailable_fn` callback from Props. Unavailable dates are
150	    /// focusable (for keyboard navigation) but not selectable.
151	    pub fn is_date_unavailable(&self, date: &CalendarDate) -> bool {
152	        if self.unavailable_dates.contains(date) {
153	            return true;
154	        }
155	        if let Some(ref predicate) = self.is_date_unavailable_fn {
156	            return predicate(date);
157	        }
158	        false
159	    }
160	
161	    /// Clamp a date into the min/max range.
162	    pub fn clamp_date(&self, date: CalendarDate) -> CalendarDate {
163	        let date = match &self.min {
164	            Some(min) if date < *min => min.clone(),
165	            _ => date,
166	        };
167	        match &self.max {
168	            Some(max) if date > *max => max.clone(),
169	            _ => date,
170	        }
171	    }
172	
173	    /// Ensure the focused date's month is visible.
174	    /// Only scrolls when the focused date falls outside all visible months.
175	    pub fn sync_visible_to_focused(&mut self) {
176	        if !self.is_in_visible_range(&self.focused_date) {
177	            self.visible_month = self.focused_date.month.get();
178	            self.visible_year = self.focused_date.year;
179	        }
180	    }
181	}
182	```
183	
184	### 1.4 Props
185	
186	```rust
187	/// Props for the Calendar component.
188	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, HasId)]
189	pub struct Props {
190	    /// Component instance ID.
191	    pub id: String,
192	    /// Controlled single-date value.
193	    pub value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>,
194	    /// Default date for uncontrolled mode.
195	    pub default_value: Option<CalendarDate>,
196	    /// Minimum selectable date.
197	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
198	    /// Maximum selectable date.
199	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
200	    /// Whether the entire calendar is non-interactive.
201	    pub disabled: bool,
202	    /// Whether the calendar allows navigation but not selection.
203	    pub readonly: bool,
204	    /// Predicate returning true for dates that should be marked unavailable.
205	    /// Unavailable dates are focusable but not selectable.
206	    pub is_date_unavailable: Option<fn(&CalendarDate) -> bool>,
207	    /// Explicit override of the locale's default first day of week.
208	    /// When `Some`, overrides the locale default. When `None`, derives from
209	    /// `ars_i18n::Locale` via `WeekInfo::first_day`.
210	    ///
211	    /// **CLDR Limitations**: The CLDR `WeekInfo::first_day` data covers most locales but
212	    /// may not account for religious or cultural contexts where the first day differs from
213	    /// the national default (e.g., Islamic calendars where Friday is the start of the week
214	    /// in religious contexts, even though Saturday is the CLDR default for `ar-SA`).
215	    /// Applications serving these contexts SHOULD use `first_day_of_week: Some(Weekday::Friday)`
216	    /// explicitly rather than relying on CLDR locale data.
217	    pub first_day_of_week: Option<Weekday>,
218	    /// Whether to display ISO week numbers at the start of each calendar row.
219	    pub show_week_numbers: bool,
220	    /// Right-to-left layout direction.
221	    pub is_rtl: bool,
222	    /// Number of months to display side-by-side. Default: 1.
223	    pub visible_months: usize,
224	    /// Controls navigation step size. Default: `PageBehavior::Visible`.
225	    pub page_behavior: PageBehavior,
226	    /// The "today" date, injected by the adapter for testability.
227	    pub today: CalendarDate,
228	}
229	
230	impl Default for Props {
231	    fn default() -> Self {
232	        Self {
233	            id: String::new(),
234	            value: None,
235	            default_value: None,
236	            min: None,
237	            max: None,
238	            disabled: false,
239	            readonly: false,
240	            is_date_unavailable: None,
241	            first_day_of_week: None,
242	            show_week_numbers: false,
243	            is_rtl: false,
244	            visible_months: 1,
245	            page_behavior: PageBehavior::Visible,
246	            today: CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025, nzu8(1), nzu8(1)),
247	        }
248	    }
249	}
250	```
251	
252	### 1.5 Guards
253	
254	```rust
255	fn is_disabled(ctx: &Context) -> bool { ctx.disabled }
256	fn is_readonly(ctx: &Context) -> bool { ctx.readonly }
257	fn is_date_disabled(ctx: &Context, date: &CalendarDate) -> bool { ctx.is_date_disabled(date) }
258	fn is_date_unavailable(ctx: &Context, date: &CalendarDate) -> bool { ctx.is_date_unavailable(date) }
259	```

265	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]

267	    /// Prev/next advances by the full `visible_months` count.

272	}

281	    pub fn week_day_labels(&self) -> Vec<(Weekday, String)> {
282	        let start = self.first_day_of_week as u8;
283	        (0..7)
284	            .map(|i| {
285	                let wd = Weekday::from_u8((start + i) % 7);
286	                let label = wd.short_label(&*self.intl_backend, &self.locale);
287	                (wd, label)
288	            })
289	            .collect()
290	    }
291	
292	    /// Build the grid for the first visible month. Equivalent to `weeks_for(0)`.
293	    pub fn weeks(&self) -> Vec<[CalendarDate; 7]> {

content.content: The file /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui/.claude/worktrees/friendly-grothendieck-886c35/spec/components/date-time/calendar.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +All `CalendarDate` arithmetic in this section uses the real `ars-i18n` API:
+`new_gregorian(year, month, day)` takes plain `u8`s and returns
+also return `Result`. Grid anchoring calls cannot fail by construction —
+the spec uses `.expect("…")` with explicit reasons where overflow is
+impossible. Adapters propagate other failures by treating the grid as

1439	
1440	**Locale-aware week start:**
1441	
1442	| Locale                                               | First Day of Week |
1443	| ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
1444	| `en-US`, `en-CA`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-TW`          | Sunday            |
1445	| `ar-SA`, `he-IL`                                     | Sunday            |
1446	| `en-GB`, `de-DE`, `fr-FR`, `es-ES`, `ru-RU`, `it-IT` | Monday            |
1447	| `fa-IR`                                              | Saturday          |
1448	
1449	The `first_day_of_week` prop overrides the locale default when provided.
1450	
1451	### 4.2 First Day of Week
1452	
1453	1. Default derived from locale via ICU4X `WeekCalculator` (US: Sunday, most of Europe: Monday, parts of Middle East: Saturday).
1454	2. Overridable via `first_day_of_week: Option<Weekday>` prop.
1455	3. In RTL locales, the calendar grid is mirrored horizontally (week starts from the right).
1456	4. DateField weekday segment typeahead matches locale-appropriate day names.
1457	
1458	### 4.3 Week Numbers
1459	
1460	1. Optional `show_week_numbers: bool` prop (default `false`).
1461	2. Week numbering follows locale convention: ISO 8601 (most of world) vs US week numbering via ICU4X `WeekCalculator`.
1462	3. Week number column header has `aria-label="Week number"` (localized).
1463	4. Each week number cell has `aria-label="Week {n}"` (localized).
1464	5. Week numbers are not focusable or selectable by default.
1465

file.content:
## 5. Variant: Multiple Selection

Calendar supports multiple non-contiguous date selection in addition to the default single-date mode.

### 5.1 Additional Props

```rust,no_check
/// Added to Props.
pub selection_mode: CalendarSelectionMode,
/// Maximum number of dates that can be selected.
pub max_selected: Option<usize>,

#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default)]
pub enum CalendarSelectionMode {
    /// Select a single date (default behavior).
    #[default]
    Single,
    /// Select multiple non-contiguous dates.
    Multiple,
}
```

### 5.2 Additional Context

```rust,no_check
/// Extended CalendarContext for multi-selection.
/// When selection_mode is Multiple:
pub selected_dates: BTreeSet<CalendarDate>,
```

### 5.3 Additional Events

```rust,no_check
/// Added to Calendar Event enum.
/// Toggle a date in/out of the selection set.
ToggleDate(CalendarDate),
```

### 5.4 Behavior

| Action   | Click                 | Shift+Click                                 |
| -------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Multiple | Toggle date selection | Select range from last-clicked to this date |

When `max_selected` is set, the `ToggleDate` transition guards against `selected_dates.len() >= max_selected`. Excess selections are silently prevented.

### 5.5 Keyboard

| Key           | Behavior                                  |
| ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| Space / Enter | Toggle focused date in/out of selection   |
| Shift+Space   | Select range from anchor to focused       |
| Ctrl+A        | Select all visible dates in current month |

## 6. Library Parity

> Compared against: React Aria (`Calendar`).

### 6.1 Props

| Feature                    | ars-ui                                | React Aria                    | Notes                                                             |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Controlled value           | `value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>` | `value: DateValue`            | Equivalent                                                        |
| Default value              | `default_value`                       | `defaultValue`                | Equivalent                                                        |
| Focused value (controlled) | --                                    | `focusedValue`                | ars-ui has `focused_date` in Context but not as a controlled prop |
| Default focused value      | --                                    | `defaultFocusedValue`         | ars-ui derives from value/today                                   |
| Min/max                    | `min`, `max`                          | `minValue`, `maxValue`        | Equivalent                                                        |
| Unavailable predicate      | `is_date_unavailable`                 | `isDateUnavailable`           | Equivalent                                                        |
| Disabled                   | `disabled`                            | `isDisabled`                  | Equivalent                                                        |
| Read-only                  | `readonly`                            | `isReadOnly`                  | Equivalent                                                        |

input.new_string:
## 5. Variant: Multiple Selection

Calendar supports multiple non-contiguous date selection alongside the
default single-date mode. The Props, Context, and Event surface for
multi-select live directly in §1.2-§1.4 (not as "additional"
declarations) — the machine is one unified type that switches behaviour
on `selection_mode` rather than two parallel `Machine` types.

### 5.1 Active surface

The unified surface relevant to multi-select:

- [`SelectionMode { Single, Multiple }`](#14-props) on `Props` and
  `Context`.
- `Props.selected_dates: Option<SelectedDates>` (controlled) and
  `Props.default_selected_dates: SelectedDates` (uncontrolled), backing
  `Context.selected_dates: Bindable<SelectedDates>`.
- `Props.max_selected: Option<usize>` and `Context.max_selected`.
- [`Event::ToggleDate { date }`](#12-events) struct variant. `SelectDate`
  is accepted in `Multiple` mode and routes to the same toggle handler so
  adapters that do not know the active mode can emit one canonical
  "select this date" event.

### 5.2 Behavior

| Action     | Click                 |
| ---------- | --------------------- |
| `Multiple` | Toggle date selection |

When `max_selected` is set, the `ToggleDate` transition guards against
`selected_dates.len() >= max_selected`. Toggles that would add a date
beyond the cap are silently dropped; toggles that remove an already-selected
date always succeed.

### 5.3 Keyboard

| Key             | Behavior                                |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `Space` / `Enter` | Toggle focused date in/out of selection |

Advanced multi-select keyboard operations (`Shift+Click`,
`Shift+Space` range fill from an anchor, `Ctrl+A` select-all visible)
are not part of this implementation. They require an "anchor" concept
not currently in the spec or machine state, and are tracked as a
follow-up that will refine §5 once a concrete anchor design lands.

content.text:
Spec port complete. Summary:

### Spec changes landed (`spec/components/date-time/calendar.md`)

601 insertions, 641 deletions. All 9 contract-changing items and the 3 cosmetic items:

| # | Change | Section |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Event::KeyDown { key, shift }` | §1.2 |
| 2 | `Event::ToggleDate { date }` struct variant | §1.2 |
| 3 | Typed `Effect::AnnounceMonth` enum; setup is marker-only (no `use_platform_effects()` in core) | §1.7 |
| 4 | Multi-select Props promoted into `§1.4 Props` | §1.4, §5.1 |
| 5 | New `SelectedDates` newtype replaces `BTreeSet<CalendarDate>` | §1.4 |
| 6 | Hover events / `is_range`/`range_start`/`range_end` / `on_cell_hover` / `on_grid_mouseleave` removed | §1.2, §1.3, §1.8 |
| 7 | `Context.unavailable_dates: Vec<CalendarDate>` removed (predicate evaluated directly) | §1.3 |
| 8 | `Context.ids: ComponentIds` replaces `heading_id`/`grid_id` string fields | §1.3, §1.8 |
| 9 | `CalendarDate` API: `u8` + `Result` returns; `.compare()` for ordering | §1.3, §1.6, §1.8 |
| 10 | `Copy + Default` derives on `PageBehavior`, `SelectionMode` | §1.4, §1.6 |
| 11 | `LocaleLabelFn` / `PageLabelFn` type aliases on `Messages` | §4.1 |
| 12 | `Props` `PartialEq` is hand-written with `core::ptr::fn_addr_eq` | §1.4 |

§5 keyboard advanced operations (Shift+Space range, Ctrl+A) now explicitly flagged as deferred pending an anchor-design follow-up.

Appendix tests rewritten to match the real API (uses `service()`/`svc.connect`, `drop()`, `KeyDown { key, shift }`, `Effect::AnnounceMonth`).

### Verification

- `cargo xtask spec validate` → **"Validated 340 files. All checks passed."**
- `cargo test -p ars-components --lib date_time::calendar` → **118 passed**

Ready to proceed with `cargo xci-fast` and the PR whenever you give the go-ahead.

lastPrompt: Port all those improvements to the spec, including the cosmetic ones

content:
# Autonomous loop check

You're being invoked on a timer while the user is away or occupied. The point is to keep work moving forward without the user driving every step — finishing things they started, maintaining PRs they're building, catching problems before they come back to find them. You're a steward, not an initiator. The user set you loose on their work, and the value you provide comes from reliably advancing things they've already set in motion, not from finding new things to do.

The key tension to navigate: the user trusts you enough to run autonomously, but that trust is easily lost. Acting on what the conversation already established is safe and valuable. Inventing new work or making irreversible changes without clear authorization erodes trust fast. When you're unsure whether something falls into "continuing established work" or "inventing new work," lean toward the former only when the transcript provides clear evidence the user wanted it done. If you find yourself reaching for justifications about why a push is probably fine, that's a signal to wait.

## What to act on

The current conversation is your highest-signal source — re-read the transcript above, since everything there is something the user was actively engaged with. The strongest signal is an in-progress PR you've been building together: review comments to address and resolve, failing CI checks to diagnose (and re-enqueue if they're flakes), merge conflicts to fix. The goal is to get the PR into a state where it's ready to merge pending only human review — the user shouldn't come back to find a PR blocked on things you could have handled. After that, look for unfinished implementation where the last exchange left something half-done, and explicit "I'll also..." or "next I'll..." commitments the conversation made and didn't honor. Weaker but still real: dangling questions you could now answer, verification steps that were skipped, edge cases that were mentioned but not handled, and natural continuations that don't require new decisions.

If you find anything in this category, act on it — actually do the work, don't describe what could be done. Run the tests, don't say "you could run the tests." The whole point of autonomous operation is that work gets done while the user is away.

When the conversation transcript has nothing left, the current branch's pull/merge request on the user's SCM is the next-best place to look. This is maintenance work — valuable, but lower priority than continuing the user's active work. Find the PR/MR for the current branch via the SCM's CLI, then check three things: CI status, unresolved review threads, and whether the branch has fallen behind the base. For failing CI, pull the failing job's logs and diagnose before acting — flaky-shaped failures (timeout, runner died, transient network) can be re-enqueued; real failures need a reproduction and a minimal fix. For unresolved review threads, fetch the comment, address the feedback, push, and resolve the thread via, for example, the GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation (or the equivalent for whichever SCM the project uses). Before pushing anything, check whether someone else has pushed to the branch while you were working — if so, rebase (don't merge) to keep history clean.

When CI is green, threads are clear, and there's idle time, sweeping the branch for issues is a good use of that time — bug-hunt or simplification passes catch problems before reviewers do, saving everyone a round-trip.

If everything is genuinely quiet — no conversation work, no PR maintenance — say so in one sentence and stop. No summary of what you checked, no list of what you might do later. The user will see your message in the transcript when they come back; three consecutive "nothing to do" results means you should scale back to a quick CI check and stop, not narrate.

## Repeated invocations

If you see earlier autonomous checks in this conversation, adjust your scope accordingly. If a previous check left a question the user hasn't answered, the cost of acting depends on reversibility: for reversible actions (local edits, running tests), make your best call and proceed; for irreversible ones (pushing, deleting, sending), keep waiting — the cost of acting wrongly on something irreversible is much higher than the cost of waiting one more cycle. If three or more consecutive checks have found nothing actionable, things are quiet — do one quick CI/threads check and stop in a single line. Repeated "nothing to do" messages clutter the transcript and waste the user's attention when they come back to review.

Read and analyze freely — understanding the state of things has no blast radius. Make edits and run tests when you're confident they continue established work. Commit and push only when you're clearly continuing something the user authorized, or when the work pattern makes the intent obvious — like fixing CI on a PR you've been building together.


---

# Autonomous loop tick (dynamic pacing)

Run the autonomous check using the loop instructions established earlier in this conversation. If you cannot find them, treat this as a no-op tick.

You scheduled this tick via the ScheduleWakeup tool (not a recurring cron). To keep the loop alive, call ScheduleWakeup again at the end of this turn with `prompt` set to the literal sentinel `<<autonomous-loop-dynamic>>` — otherwise the loop ends after this tick.

If a Monitor is armed (check TaskList), keep `delaySeconds` at 1200–1800s — the Monitor is the wake signal and this is only the fallback heartbeat. If you were woken by a `<task-notification>`, handle the event before rescheduling. To stop the loop, also TaskStop the monitor (use TaskList to find its task ID if no longer in context).

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

attachment.content:
=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

1	//! `Calendar` component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! Framework-agnostic implementation of the grid-based date picker defined
4	//! in `spec/components/date-time/calendar.md`. The machine owns visible
5	//! month/year, focused-date roving tab state, single and multi-date
6	//! selection, locale-aware weekday ordering, min/max constraints, and the
7	//! screen-reader announcement intent emitted when the visible month
8	//! changes.
9	//!
10	//! ## Spec-vs-implementation reconciliation
11	//!
12	//! The spec's §1.7 code examples use illustrative pseudo-Rust that
13	//! pre-dates the current `ars-i18n::CalendarDate` API: it treats months and
14	//! days as `NonZero<u8>`, calls `add_days` / `add_months` as infallible,
15	//! and accesses month/day/year as fields. The real types return
16	//! `Result<_, CalendarError>` and expose month/day/year as `u8` methods.
17	//! The implementation here keeps the spec's semantics intact while using
18	//! the real fallible API; cases where the spec implies an arithmetic
19	//! result cannot fail (grid anchoring, focused-date navigation) propagate
20	//! using `?`-style early returns that leave context unchanged rather than
21	//! panic at calendar-system boundaries.
22	//!
23	//! ## Range-calendar drift in spec §1.8
24	//!
25	//! The spec's §1.8 connect API includes `on_cell_hover(date) ->
26	//! Event::HoverDate { date }` and `on_grid_mouseleave() ->
27	//! Event::HoverEnd`, but neither event appears in §1.2 and neither has a
28	//! transition arm. Those entries belong to the future `RangeCalendar`
29	//! component; this implementation omits them and the matching
30	//! `Event::HoverDate` / `Event::HoverEnd` variants. The range-calendar
31	//! delivery will reintroduce them alongside the rest of its hover/range
32	//! preview state.
33	
34	pub mod grid;
35	
36	#[cfg(test)]
37	mod tests;
38	
39	use alloc::{
40	    format,
41	    string::{String, ToString as _},
42	    sync::Arc,
43	    vec::Vec,
44	};
45	use core::{
46	    cmp::Ordering,
47	    fmt::{self, Debug},
48	};
49	
50	use ars_core::{
51	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env,
52	    HtmlAttr, IntlBackend, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, TransitionPlan,
53	};
54	use ars_i18n::{CalendarDate, Weekday};
55	
56	use self::grid::{
57	    advance_month as advance_month_pair, is_in_visible_range as grid_is_in_visible_range,
58	    is_outside_month_at_offset as grid_is_outside_month_at_offset,
59	    month_year_at_offset as grid_month_year_at_offset, ordered_weekdays as grid_ordered_weekdays,
60	    weeks_for as grid_weeks_for,
61	};
62	
63	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64	// State / Event / Effect
65	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	
67	/// States for the `Calendar` component.
68	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
69	pub enum State {
70	    /// Calendar rendered but no cell has keyboard focus.
71	    Idle,
72	
73	    /// A specific date cell holds keyboard focus within the grid.
74	    Focused,
75	}
76	
77	/// Events for the `Calendar` component.
78	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
79	pub enum Event {
80	    /// Move keyboard focus to a specific date (clamped to min/max).
81	    FocusDate {
82	        /// The date to focus on.
83	        date: CalendarDate,
84	    },
85	
86	    /// User selected a date via click or `Enter`/`Space` on the focused cell.
87	    ///
88	    /// In [`SelectionMode::Single`] this sets the value to that date. In
89	    /// [`SelectionMode::Multiple`] it falls through to [`Event::ToggleDate`]
90	    /// to keep one user-visible "select this date" event for adapters that
91	    /// do not know the configured selection mode.
92	    SelectDate {
93	        /// The date that was selected.
94	        date: CalendarDate,
95	    },
96	
97	    /// Toggle the date in or out of the multi-selection set.
98	    ///
99	    /// Only meaningful when `selection_mode == Multiple`. A toggle that
100	    /// would add a date when the set already holds `max_selected` entries
101	    /// is silently dropped.
102	    ToggleDate {
103	        /// The date to toggle.
104	        date: CalendarDate,
105	    },
106	
107	    /// Navigate to the next month (advances by `page_behavior` step).
108	    NextMonth,
109	
110	    /// Navigate to the previous month (retreats by `page_behavior` step).
111	    PrevMonth,
112	
113	    /// Navigate forward by 12 months (one year).
114	    NextYear,
115	
116	    /// Navigate backward by 12 months (one year).
117	    PrevYear,
118	
119	    /// Jump to a specific month (1-based; out-of-range values are ignored).
120	    SetMonth {
121	        /// The 1-based month to display.
122	        month: u8,
123	    },
124	
125	    /// Jump to a specific year.
126	    SetYear {
127	        /// The year to display.
128	        year: i32,
129	    },
130	
131	    /// Grid received focus.
132	    FocusIn,
133	
134	    /// Focus left the grid entirely.
135	    FocusOut,
136	
137	    /// Keyboard event on the grid.
138	    ///
139	    /// The `shift` flag is folded into the event so the adapter does not
140	    /// need a side-channel to communicate `Shift+PageUp` / `Shift+PageDown`
141	    /// for year navigation.
142	    KeyDown {
143	        /// The key that was pressed.
144	        key: KeyboardKey,
145	
146	        /// Whether the shift modifier was held.
147	        shift: bool,
148	    },
149	}
150	
151	/// Typed identifier for every named effect intent the `calendar` machine emits.
152	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
153	pub enum Effect {
154	    /// Announce the newly visible month (or month range) to assistive tech
155	    /// via the adapter's platform announcer. Triggered by every
156	    /// month/year navigation transition.
157	    AnnounceMonth,
158	}
159	
160	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161	// Props sub-types
162	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	/// Controls how prev/next navigation advances when multiple months are visible.
165	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
166	pub enum PageBehavior {
167	    /// Prev/next advances by the full `visible_months` count.
168	    #[default]
169	    Visible,
170	
171	    /// Prev/next advances by exactly 1 month, regardless of `visible_months`.
172	    Single,
173	}
174	
175	/// Whether the calendar selects a single date or an unordered set of dates.
176	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
177	pub enum SelectionMode {
178	    /// Single-date selection — `value` holds the chosen date (or `None`).
179	    #[default]
180	    Single,
181	
182	    /// Multiple non-contiguous date selection — `selected_dates` holds the
183	    /// chosen set, optionally capped by `max_selected`.
184	    Multiple,
185	}
186	
187	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
188	// Props
189	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
190	
191	/// Ordered set of unique calendar dates, sorted by
192	/// [`CalendarDate::compare`].
193	///
194	/// [`CalendarDate`] implements neither [`Ord`] nor [`PartialOrd`] (different
195	/// calendar systems share the same `CalendarDate` value type, so global
196	/// ordering is undefined), but it does expose a chronological
197	/// [`compare`](CalendarDate::compare) method. `SelectedDates` is a thin
198	/// newtype wrapper around `Vec<CalendarDate>` that maintains sorted order
199	/// using that comparator, plays the role of `SelectedDates` for the
200	/// multi-select variant, and stays usable without any extra trait impls on
201	/// the foreign type.
202	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
203	pub struct SelectedDates {
204	    dates: Vec<CalendarDate>,
205	}
206	
207	impl SelectedDates {
208	    /// Creates an empty set.
209	    #[must_use]
210	    pub const fn new() -> Self {
211	        Self { dates: Vec::new() }
212	    }
213	
214	    /// Returns the number of selected dates.
215	    #[must_use]
216	    pub const fn len(&self) -> usize {
217	        self.dates.len()
218	    }
219

-/// `PartialEq` is implemented by hand rather than derived because
-/// `is_date_unavailable` is a raw function pointer; the auto-derived
-/// `PartialEq` would compare those pointers directly and surface a

input.old_string:
impl PartialEq for Props {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        let predicates_eq = match (self.is_date_unavailable, other.is_date_unavailable) {
            (None, None) => true,
            (Some(a), Some(b)) => core::ptr::fn_addr_eq(a, b),
            _ => false,
        };

        self.id == other.id
            && self.value == other.value
            && self.default_value == other.default_value
            && self.selected_dates == other.selected_dates
            && self.default_selected_dates == other.default_selected_dates
            && self.selection_mode == other.selection_mode
            && self.max_selected == other.max_selected
            && self.min == other.min
            && self.max == other.max
            && self.disabled == other.disabled
            && self.readonly == other.readonly
            && predicates_eq
            && self.first_day_of_week == other.first_day_of_week
            && self.show_week_numbers == other.show_week_numbers
            && self.is_rtl == other.is_rtl
            && self.visible_months == other.visible_months
            && self.page_behavior == other.page_behavior
            && self.today == other.today
    }
}

impl PartialEq for Props {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        let predicates_eq = match (self.is_date_unavailable, other.is_date_unavailable) {
            (None, None) => true,
            (Some(a), Some(b)) => core::ptr::fn_addr_eq(a, b),
            _ => false,
        };

        self.id == other.id
            && self.value == other.value
            && self.default_value == other.default_value
            && self.selected_dates == other.selected_dates
            && self.default_selected_dates == other.default_selected_dates
            && self.selection_mode == other.selection_mode
            && self.max_selected == other.max_selected
            && self.min == other.min
            && self.max == other.max
            && self.disabled == other.disabled
            && self.readonly == other.readonly
            && predicates_eq
            && self.first_day_of_week == other.first_day_of_week
            && self.show_week_numbers == other.show_week_numbers
            && self.is_rtl == other.is_rtl
            && self.visible_months == other.visible_months
            && self.page_behavior == other.page_behavior
            && self.today == other.today
    }
}

attachment.content:
=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

impl PartialEq for Props {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        let predicates_eq = match (self.is_date_unavailable, other.is_date_unavailable) {
            (None, None) => true,
            (Some(a), Some(b)) => core::ptr::fn_addr_eq(a, b),
            _ => false,
        };

        self.id == other.id
            && self.value == other.value
            && self.default_value == other.default_value
            && self.selected_dates == other.selected_dates
            && self.default_selected_dates == other.default_selected_dates
            && self.selection_mode == other.selection_mode
            && self.max_selected == other.max_selected
            && self.min == other.min
            && self.max == other.max
            && self.disabled == other.disabled
            && self.readonly == other.readonly
            && predicates_eq
            && self.first_day_of_week == other.first_day_of_week
            && self.show_week_numbers == other.show_week_numbers
            && self.is_rtl == other.is_rtl
            && self.visible_months == other.visible_months
            && self.page_behavior == other.page_behavior
            && self.today == other.today
    }
}

259	    Single,

261	}
262	
263	/// Predicate type for marking dates unavailable.
264	///
265	/// A bare `fn` pointer (not a closure) keeps `Props` `Clone + PartialEq`
266	/// without bespoke comparison logic; applications that need richer
267	/// per-instance predicates can route through a static pointer-stable
268	/// function.
269	pub type IsDateUnavailableFn = fn(&CalendarDate) -> bool;
270	
271	/// Props for the Calendar component.
272	///
273	/// `PartialEq` is implemented by hand rather than derived because
274	/// `is_date_unavailable` is a raw function pointer; the auto-derived
275	/// `PartialEq` would compare those pointers directly and surface a
276	/// `unpredictable_function_pointer_comparisons` lint. The manual impl uses
277	/// `core::ptr::fn_addr_eq` for predicate equality and field-wise equality
278	/// everywhere else.
279	#[derive(Clone, Debug, HasId)]
280	pub struct Props {
281	    /// Component instance ID.
282	    pub id: String,
283	    /// Controlled single-date value.
284	    pub value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>,
285	    /// Default date for uncontrolled single-select mode.
286	    pub default_value: Option<CalendarDate>,
287	    /// Controlled multi-selection set.
288	    pub selected_dates: Option<SelectedDates>,
289	    /// Default selection set for uncontrolled multi-select mode.
290	    pub default_selected_dates: SelectedDates,
291	    /// Selection mode — single by default. Multi-select unlocks
292	    /// `selected_dates` and the `ToggleDate` event.
293	    pub selection_mode: SelectionMode,
294	    /// Maximum number of dates that can be selected in `Multiple` mode.
295	    /// `None` removes the cap; excess toggles are silently dropped.
296	    pub max_selected: Option<usize>,
297	    /// Minimum selectable date.
298	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
299	    /// Maximum selectable date.
300	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
301	    /// Whether the entire calendar is non-interactive.
302	    pub disabled: bool,
303	    /// Whether the calendar allows navigation but not selection.
304	    pub readonly: bool,
305	    /// Predicate returning true for dates that should be marked unavailable.
306	    /// Unavailable dates are focusable but not selectable.
307	    pub is_date_unavailable: Option<IsDateUnavailableFn>,
308	    /// Explicit override of the locale's default first day of week.
309	    /// When `Some`, overrides the locale default. When `None`, derives from
310	    /// `ars_i18n::Locale` via `WeekInfo::first_day`.
311	    ///
312	    /// **CLDR Limitations**: The CLDR `WeekInfo::first_day` data covers most locales but
313	    /// may not account for religious or cultural contexts where the first day differs from
314	    /// the national default (e.g., Islamic calendars where Friday is the start of the week
315	    /// in religious contexts, even though Saturday is the CLDR default for `ar-SA`).
316	    /// Applications serving these contexts SHOULD use `first_day_of_week: Some(Weekday::Friday)`
317	    /// explicitly rather than relying on CLDR locale data.
318	    pub first_day_of_week: Option<Weekday>,
319	    /// Whether to display ISO week numbers at the start of each calendar row.
320	    pub show_week_numbers: bool,
321	    /// Right-to-left layout direction.
322	    pub is_rtl: bool,
323	    /// Number of months to display side-by-side. Values below 1 are
324	    /// clamped to 1 by `Machine::init`. Default: 1.
325	    pub visible_months: usize,
326	    /// Controls navigation step size. Default: `PageBehavior::Visible`.
327	    pub page_behavior: PageBehavior,
328	    /// The "today" date, injected by the adapter for testability.
329	    pub today: CalendarDate,
330	}
331	
332	impl PartialEq for Props {
333	    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
334	        let predicates_eq = match (self.is_date_unavailable, other.is_date_unavailable) {
335	            (None, None) => true,
336	            (Some(a), Some(b)) => core::ptr::fn_addr_eq(a, b),
337	            _ => false,
338	        };
339	        // … then field-wise equality across every remaining field.
340	        predicates_eq
341	            && self.id == other.id
342	            && self.value == other.value
343	            // …
344	    }
345	}
346	
347	impl Default for Props {
348	    fn default() -> Self {
349	        Self {
350	            id: String::new(),
351	            value: None,
352	            default_value: None,
353	            selected_dates: None,
354	            default_selected_dates: SelectedDates::new(),
355	            selection_mode: SelectionMode::Single,
356	            max_selected: None,
357	            min: None,

|---|---|

### Changes for this round

- [crates/ars-core/src/callback.rs](crates/ars-core/src/callback.rs) — adds the `new_ref` constructor on `Callback<dyn for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out + Send + Sync>` plus two unit tests (`callback_new_ref_invokes_higher_ranked_closure`, `callback_new_ref_supports_captured_state`).
- [crates/ars-components/src/date_time/calendar/mod.rs](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/calendar/mod.rs) — `IsDateUnavailableFn` is now `Callback<dyn for<'a> Fn(&'a CalendarDate) -> bool + Send + Sync>`. Manual `Props::PartialEq` deleted; back to `#[derive(PartialEq)]`. `Context::is_date_unavailable_fn` field is now `Option<Callback<...>>` with `.as_ref()` for the predicate call.
- [crates/ars-components/src/date_time/calendar/tests.rs](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/calendar/tests.rs) — four test sites now use `Callback::new_ref(|d: &CalendarDate| …)` (closures with captured state, e.g., the equality test demonstrates `Arc::ptr_eq` semantics).
- [spec/components/date-time/calendar.md](spec/components/date-time/calendar.md) — `IsDateUnavailableFn` doc updated, manual `PartialEq` block removed, Props goes back to deriving `PartialEq`.

Ready for `cargo xci-fast` + push whenever you give the go-ahead.

1	//! `Calendar` component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! Framework-agnostic implementation of the grid-based date picker defined
4	//! in `spec/components/date-time/calendar.md`. The machine owns visible
5	//! month/year, focused-date roving tab state, single and multi-date
6	//! selection, locale-aware weekday ordering, min/max constraints, and the
7	//! screen-reader announcement intent emitted when the visible month
8	//! changes.
9	//!
10	//! ## Spec-vs-implementation reconciliation
11	//!
12	//! The spec's §1.7 code examples use illustrative pseudo-Rust that
13	//! pre-dates the current `ars-i18n::CalendarDate` API: it treats months and
14	//! days as `NonZero<u8>`, calls `add_days` / `add_months` as infallible,
15	//! and accesses month/day/year as fields. The real types return
16	//! `Result<_, CalendarError>` and expose month/day/year as `u8` methods.
17	//! The implementation here keeps the spec's semantics intact while using
18	//! the real fallible API; cases where the spec implies an arithmetic
19	//! result cannot fail (grid anchoring, focused-date navigation) propagate
20	//! using `?`-style early returns that leave context unchanged rather than
21	//! panic at calendar-system boundaries.
22	//!
23	//! ## Range-calendar drift in spec §1.8
24	//!
25	//! The spec's §1.8 connect API includes `on_cell_hover(date) ->
26	//! Event::HoverDate { date }` and `on_grid_mouseleave() ->
27	//! Event::HoverEnd`, but neither event appears in §1.2 and neither has a
28	//! transition arm. Those entries belong to the future `RangeCalendar`
29	//! component; this implementation omits them and the matching
30	//! `Event::HoverDate` / `Event::HoverEnd` variants. The range-calendar
31	//! delivery will reintroduce them alongside the rest of its hover/range
32	//! preview state.
33	
34	pub mod grid;
35	
36	#[cfg(test)]
37	mod tests;
38	
39	use alloc::{
40	    format,
41	    string::{String, ToString as _},
42	    sync::Arc,
43	    vec::Vec,
44	};
45	use core::{
46	    cmp::Ordering,
47	    fmt::{self, Debug},
48	};
49	
50	use ars_core::{
51	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart,
52	    ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, IntlBackend, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, TransitionPlan,
53	};
54	use ars_i18n::{CalendarDate, Weekday};
55	
56	use self::grid::{
57	    advance_month as advance_month_pair, is_in_visible_range as grid_is_in_visible_range,
58	    is_outside_month_at_offset as grid_is_outside_month_at_offset,
59	    month_year_at_offset as grid_month_year_at_offset, ordered_weekdays as grid_ordered_weekdays,
60	    weeks_for as grid_weeks_for,
61	};
62	
63	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64	// State / Event / Effect
65	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	
67	/// States for the `Calendar` component.
68	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
69	pub enum State {
70	    /// Calendar rendered but no cell has keyboard focus.
71	    Idle,
72	
73	    /// A specific date cell holds keyboard focus within the grid.
74	    Focused,
75	}
76	
77	/// Events for the `Calendar` component.
78	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
79	pub enum Event {
80	    /// Move keyboard focus to a specific date (clamped to min/max).
81	    FocusDate {
82	        /// The date to focus on.
83	        date: CalendarDate,
84	    },
85	
86	    /// User selected a date via click or `Enter`/`Space` on the focused cell.
87	    ///
88	    /// In [`SelectionMode::Single`] this sets the value to that date. In
89	    /// [`SelectionMode::Multiple`] it falls through to [`Event::ToggleDate`]
90	    /// to keep one user-visible "select this date" event for adapters that
91	    /// do not know the configured selection mode.
92	    SelectDate {
93	        /// The date that was selected.
94	        date: CalendarDate,
95	    },
96	
97	    /// Toggle the date in or out of the multi-selection set.
98	    ///
99	    /// Only meaningful when `selection_mode == Multiple`. A toggle that
100	    /// would add a date when the set already holds `max_selected` entries
101	    /// is silently dropped.
102	    ToggleDate {
103	        /// The date to toggle.
104	        date: CalendarDate,
105	    },
106	
107	    /// Navigate to the next month (advances by `page_behavior` step).
108	    NextMonth,
109	
110	    /// Navigate to the previous month (retreats by `page_behavior` step).
111	    PrevMonth,
112	
113	    /// Navigate forward by 12 months (one year).
114	    NextYear,
115	
116	    /// Navigate backward by 12 months (one year).
117	    PrevYear,
118	
119	    /// Jump to a specific month (1-based; out-of-range values are ignored).
120	    SetMonth {
121	        /// The 1-based month to display.
122	        month: u8,
123	    },
124	
125	    /// Jump to a specific year.
126	    SetYear {
127	        /// The year to display.
128	        year: i32,
129	    },
130	
131	    /// Grid received focus.
132	    FocusIn,
133	
134	    /// Focus left the grid entirely.
135	    FocusOut,
136	
137	    /// Keyboard event on the grid.
138	    ///
139	    /// The `shift` flag is folded into the event so the adapter does not
140	    /// need a side-channel to communicate `Shift+PageUp` / `Shift+PageDown`
141	    /// for year navigation.
142	    KeyDown {
143	        /// The key that was pressed.
144	        key: KeyboardKey,
145	
146	        /// Whether the shift modifier was held.
147	        shift: bool,
148	    },
149	}
150	
151	/// Typed identifier for every named effect intent the `calendar` machine emits.
152	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
153	pub enum Effect {
154	    /// Announce the newly visible month (or month range) to assistive tech
155	    /// via the adapter's platform announcer. Triggered by every
156	    /// month/year navigation transition.
157	    AnnounceMonth,
158	}
159	
160	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161	// Props sub-types
162	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	/// Controls how prev/next navigation advances when multiple months are visible.
165	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
166	pub enum PageBehavior {
167	    /// Prev/next advances by the full `visible_months` count.
168	    #[default]
169	    Visible,
170	
171	    /// Prev/next advances by exactly 1 month, regardless of `visible_months`.
172	    Single,
173	}
174	
175	/// Whether the calendar selects a single date or an unordered set of dates.
176	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
177	pub enum SelectionMode {
178	    /// Single-date selection — `value` holds the chosen date (or `None`).
179	    #[default]
180	    Single,
181	
182	    /// Multiple non-contiguous date selection — `selected_dates` holds the
183	    /// chosen set, optionally capped by `max_selected`.
184	    Multiple,
185	}
186	
187	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
188	// Props
189	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
190	
191	/// Ordered set of unique calendar dates, sorted by
192	/// [`CalendarDate::compare`].
193	///
194	/// [`CalendarDate`] implements neither [`Ord`] nor [`PartialOrd`] (different
195	/// calendar systems share the same `CalendarDate` value type, so global
196	/// ordering is undefined), but it does expose a chronological
197	/// [`compare`](CalendarDate::compare) method. `SelectedDates` is a thin
198	/// newtype wrapper around `Vec<CalendarDate>` that maintains sorted order
199	/// using that comparator, plays the role of `SelectedDates` for the
200	/// multi-select variant, and stays usable without any extra trait impls on
201	/// the foreign type.
202	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
203	pub struct SelectedDates {
204	    dates: Vec<CalendarDate>,
205	}
206	
207	impl SelectedDates {
208	    /// Creates an empty set.

210	    pub const fn new() -> Self {
211	        Self { dates: Vec::new() }
212	    }
213	
214	    /// Returns the number of selected dates.
215	    #[must_use]
216	    pub const fn len(&self) -> usize {
217	        self.dates.len()
218	    }

When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text

===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement Calendar agnostic core (#280)

Adds the framework-agnostic Calendar state machine, grid math, and connect
API in `ars-components::date_time::calendar`, plus the workspace-level
extensions and spec sync that fall out of the implementation:

- New `Callback::new_ref` constructor in `ars-core` accepting higher-ranked
  closures over a reference argument (`for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out`). Lets
  predicate-style Props fields (here `Calendar::is_date_unavailable`) use
  `Callback` instead of falling back to raw `fn` pointers.

- Spec `spec/components/date-time/calendar.md` updated end-to-end:
  - `Event::KeyDown { key, shift }` folds the shift modifier into the
    transition (no side-channel adapter dispatch).
  - `Event::ToggleDate { date }` as a struct variant; `SelectDate` routes
    through the active selection mode.
  - Typed `Effect::AnnounceMonth` enum replaces the bare string name;
    setup is marker-only — adapters resolve the platform announce.
  - Multi-select Props promoted into §1.4 unified Props
    (`selection_mode`, `max_selected`, `selected_dates`,
    `default_selected_dates`).
  - New `SelectedDates` newtype replaces `BTreeSet<CalendarDate>`
    (`CalendarDate` has no `Ord`; the wrapper keeps a sorted `Vec`
    via `CalendarDate::compare`).
  - Range-calendar drift removed: `Event::HoverDate`/`HoverEnd`,
    `Context::is_range`/`range_start`/`range_end`, `on_cell_hover`,
    `on_grid_mouseleave`. Those belong to the future RangeCalendar.
  - `Context::unavailable_dates` cache field removed (predicate evaluated
    directly).
  - `Context::ids: ComponentIds` replaces the `heading_id`/`grid_id`
    string fields; all part IDs derive from `ids.part(...)`.
  - `CalendarDate` API form realigned: `u8` not `NonZero<u8>`,
    `Result<_, CalendarError>` propagation, `.compare()` for ordering.
  - `Copy + Default` on `PageBehavior` and `SelectionMode`.
  - `LocaleLabelFn` / `PageLabelFn` type aliases for the `Messages`
    closures.

- §5.5 advanced multi-select keyboard ops (`Shift+Space` range from anchor,
  `Ctrl+A` select-all) are explicitly out of scope — they require an
  anchor design the spec does not yet define. Tracked as follow-up.

- Tests: 118 unit+snapshot in `calendar/tests.rs`, calendar anatomy
  in spec_conformance, three nightly-only proptests (single-select,
  multi-select, page-behavior arithmetic). 27 committed `.snap`
  fixtures. Two new `ars-core` unit tests cover `Callback::new_ref`.

- Coverage on the new module sits at ≥95% line and ≥95% branch for both
  `calendar/mod.rs` and `calendar/grid.rs`. Mutation testing on the
  module saw 0 missed across multiple partial runs.

Closes #280

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10
